Sub-product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, my wife and I reserved a rental car from a rental car company in the XXXX, XXXX for the rental dates of XXXX XXXX XXXX  We used our Chase Sapphire Reserve card to make the reservation. Accordingly, on XX/XX/XXXX, a charge of {$75.00} ( XXXX XXXX ) appeared on our XXXX credit card statement. In addition, we have a copy of the initial rental agreement, indicating that our Chase card was used to make the reservation. 

On XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX we picked up the rental car at the XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX in the XXXX, XXXX. In reliance upon the instructions provided under the Chase Guide to Benefits, we declined the rental car companys collision damage waiver in order to use the Chase Auto Rental Coverage as our primary insurance. The Chase Guide to Benefits states that in order to use the coverage provided by the card we must decline the rental companys collision damage waiver ( XXXX  ) option or a similar provision. As a result, we have no other insurance coverage applicable to this incident. 

On XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX we suffered a flat tire after colliding with an unidentified object in the road. The damage to the tire could not be repaired and the rental car company advised that the tire would need to be replaced. The rental company provided an incident report documenting the incident and deducted the amount owed for the damage -- {$350.00} ( XXXX XXXX ) -- from the hold on our Chase card. On XX/XX/XXXX, a charge of {$350.00} ( XXXX XXXX ) appears on our XXXX credit card statement. In addition we have a copy of the final rental agreement, indicating that our Chase card was used to was used to pay this charge. 

All of the documentation cited above, including the XXXX and XXXX statements, initial and final rental agreements, and incident report were provided to the company contracted by Chase to process our insurance claim ( Assurant ). This documentation is more than sufficient to establish that we complied with the terms for obtaining coverage as set forth in the Guide to Benefits. Specifically, we used our card to make the reservation and we have documentation that proves the car suffered damage, which we covered out-of-pocket. The information made available by Chase states that Chase provides primary coverage, including for damage to the vehicle. 

Subsequent to filing our claim, we have repeatedly called Chase benefits administrators regarding the status of our claim and, depending on the administrator, we have variously been told that we need to : ( 1 ) upload the declarations page of our personal car insurance policy ; ( 2 ) sign a statement indicating with have no other applicable insurance ; ( 3 ) directly email the insurance adjuster responsible for our claim ( who does not respond ) ; and ( 4 ) provide a copy of a " demand letter '' ( which the rental company did not issue due to the amount being deducted from the hold on our card ). Most recently we were advised by a benefits administrator that the coverage does not apply to flat tires ( despite no such exclusion appearing in the insurance agreement ). On XX/XX/XXXX, Assurant sent a form letter stating that our claim was denied because " the documentation provided does not substantiate the loss being claimed. '' This is the second time we have sought to use our Chase Sapphire Reserve insurance benefits. A prior claim resulting from delayed luggage was never paid, following a similar cycle of repeated requests for additional documentation no person could reasonably be expected to provide. It appears these requests for additional documentation may be part of an intentional tactic to delay and, ultimately, deny payment of claims.

Consumer complaint narrative:
Full detail provided in below attached PDF document. 
I accessed my free Experian credit report on XXXX XXXX, 2015. Prior to that date I had last looked at my credit report for refinancing my home a couple years prior. In the past XXXX years my credit report has reflected a FICO score of XXXX or higher, and is something I take very seriously and work hard to maintain. Thus when I saw that my credit report showed a past due account for which I was totally unaware, and which adversely impacted my FICO score, I inquired with Experian for detailed information on this past due account as I assume that it is incorrectly posted to muy account. I sent Experian a consumer inquiry via their webiste input screens as I assumed that the past due account was incorrectly posted to my account as I had never heard of the reporting company - XXXX XXXX XXXX . However, after XXXX days Experian reported via their website that regarding my inquiry, the " outcome remained the same '', which appeared to be a canned consumer response. I question seriously if anything was done at all, as I would expect XXXX to contact me regarding my inquiry so that we could rectify this incorrect posting. 
I then wrote a letter, attached below, and mailed it to both Experian and to XXXX regarding this past due account and in this letter notified them that I would wait XXXX days for them to respond and would then come to the CFPB consumer complaint portal to file a complaint. Today I placed XXXX more electronic complaint on the Experian website, where I attached the below letter. I have been unable to contact XXXX or to receive any communication what so ever from XXXX regarding this complaint. 
My main reason for this consumer complaint is to protest that XXXX is allowed to provide a past due notificaiton to my credit report without ever having made any contact with me what so ever. I have never had any communications with this Progressive company, have never spoken to anyone from this company, have never received anythign in the mail from this company, and when I inquired about this company to Experian, I have recieved XXXX information to allow me to investigate either this company of this past due account. 
Thus the reasons for my consumer complaint are, 1. ) I wonder how many other consumer accounts are being incorrectly reported by XXXX with XXXX consumer communications prior to these postings, and 2. ) I wonder if Experian has any guidelines for reporting companies or requirements that reporting companies show proof of consumer correspondence for such harmful postings. 

As in many cases, communications is the most important issue here and most consumers probably do not know about the CFPB consumer complaint portal, and are not as persistent as I have been. I am amazed that Progressive has received XXXX inquiry regarding my account from Experian and a XXXX inquiry from me and still I have recieved XXXX communications at all from this company which I have never heard of. I would believe that companies such as XXXX who are allowed to post derogatory credit reporting information against consumer accounts would be required to meet consumer contact requirements prior to making such harmful postings. The assistance of the CFPB in this matter is greatly appreciated. Thank you. 
P.S. Please note that I have selected above that this past due credit reporting comment is not mine, and while I certaily believe this to be the case, my complaint is that I can not receive any background response what-so-ever into this issue to prove if it is or ever was a valid account or who it really does belong to.

Consumer complaint narrative:
To whom this may concern, I am writing this letter as a long time TD Bank Customer. I believe my first account was opened up with the assistance of my parents as a young teenager and I have been banking with them ever since. I have held both a Personal Savings Account as well as a Personal Checking Account and for the last 5 years have held a TD Bank Credit Card as well. 
On XX/XX/2023 I noticed there were unauthorized purchases made using my TD Bank Credit Card. There were multiple unauthorized purchases made using my card. I immediately called TD Bank Card Services to report this. I was told these unauthorized purchases would be taken care of. During that conversation on XX/XX/2023 the current credit card I had was cancelled and I was told I would be issued a new card and that it would arrive in approximately 7-14 days. 
On XX/XX/2023 I noticed that my auto-payment that I had set to pay my monthly balance on the card did not go through so therefore I received late fees and interest charges. On that date I also noticed two other unauthorized purchases. I called TD Card Services again to report these new finding and concerns. And again I was told this would be taken care of. 
After my initial two phone calls I placed one more call on XX/XX/2023 to follow up as I did not see the late fees or interest charges removed from my card. And again I was told that it would be taken care of and that it would be reflected in my account within the next 2 business days. 
Fast forward to XX/XX/2023 I receive a letter in the mail stating my TD Bank Credit Card account has been put into collections. I call the phone number listed on this letter and am explained that I have not been issued a new credit card and will not be issued a new credit card due to being placed in collections. No one could explain why my account was placed in collections and they were requesting a {$6.00} FEE to remove my account from collections. They were unable to explain the fees or outstanding balance. 
It is now XX/XX/2023 and this situation is still not resolved. I have made five total phone calls and two in person visits to a TD Bank Branch. There are still two unauthorized purchases on my account that are listed in my monthly statement. At this point, I believe that TD Bank has not acted in accordance with Regulation EI have continually been told that these items will be taken care of and they have not been. I have not received any formal response from TD Bank regarding these disputed transactions, either approving or denying my claim of Unauthorized charged as described under Regulation E.

I am a patient and understanding person but this is an extremely inconvenient situation that is having a negative impact on my daily life along with my good standing credit. I have decided after nearly XXXX  decades as a TD Bank Customer, I will no longer be banking with them.

And according to the fair credit reporting act Whenever a consumer reporting agency prepares a consumer report it shall follow reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy of the information concerning the individual about whom the report relates according to 15 USC 1681 ( e ) ( b ).

And according to 15 USC 1681 5 they are suppose to do this ( 5 ) Treatment of inaccurate or unverifiable information ( A ) In generallf, after any reinvestigation under paragraph ( 1 ) of any information disputed by a consumer, an item of the information is found to be inaccurate or incomplete or can not be verified, the consumer reporting agency shall ( i ) promptly delete that item of information from the file of the consumer, or modify that item of information, as appropriate, based on the results of the reinvestigation; and ( ii ) promptly notify the furnisher of that information that the information has been modified or deleted from the file of the consumer.

And according to 15 USC 1681s-2 they are suppose to not furnish any information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if the person knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate.

If you can not validate this debt, you are required to : Delete all references to this account from every consumer reporting agency to which it has been reported ( including XXXX  and XXXX  ).

I also reserve the right to pursue legal remedies under the FDCPA and the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ).

If the information can not be verified within 30 days, it must be removed from the report. I demand verification of this account, including supporting documents that prove this debt is mine. Failure to provide such proof requires that you delete the account from my file.

I request that this matter be resolved promptly. Furthermore, these specific accounts have already been removed by other major credit bureaus after failing verification, which underscores the inaccuracy of their reporting.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX CA XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX TransUnion Consumer Solutions XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, PA XXXX XXXX : Follow-Up Regarding Unauthorized Inquiries on My Credit Report To Whom It May Concern, I am writing in response to your recent communication regarding my dispute of unauthorized inquiries on my credit report. While I appreciate your explanation that inquiries remain on my report for two years, your response does not address the issue at hand : the verification of permissible purpose for the inquiries I disputed.

As stated in my original letter, under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) 15 U.S.C. 1681b, a consumer report may only be accessed for specific permissible purposes, and such purposes must be documented. Additionally, 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) requires that information found to be inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable after a reinvestigation must be promptly deleted. 

I have already contacted the companies responsible for these inquiries, and they have failed to provide written authorization or any documentation demonstrating I initiated or authorized these inquiries. This failure makes the inquiries unverifiable and, therefore, subject to removal under the FCRA. 

To reiterate, the inquiries in question are as follows : XXXX XXXX Inquiry Date : XX/XX/XXXX | Removal Date : XX/XX/XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX Inquiry Date : XX/XX/XXXX | Removal Date : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX Inquiry Date : XX/XX/XXXX | Removal Date : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX Inquiry Date : XX/XX/XXXX | Removal Date : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX Inquiry Date : XX/XX/XXXX | Removal Date : XX/XX/XXXX I respectfully request that you provide me with documentation confirming these inquiries were made with permissible purpose. If you are unable to provide such evidence, I ask that these inquiries be removed from my credit report immediately. 

Please provide a written response to this matter, including the results of your reinvestigation, within 30 days as required by law. If no resolution is reached, I will escalate this matter to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) and consider further legal action to protect my rights under the FCRA. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Furthermore, under 15 U.S. Code 1681i ( 5 ) Treatment of Inaccurate or Unverifiable Information, you are required to promptly delete or modify any information found to be inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable during a reinvestigation. I have put a credit freeze in effect and have made a report with the FTC.

Consumer complaint narrative:
To whom this may concern, There are 2 related but separate complaints regarding the XXXX XXXX Credit card product. 
First Complaint : I 'm a long time Citi credit card holder. In XX/XX/XXXX 2015, I upgraded from their Citi XXXX Signature card to their {$95.00} annual fee Citi XXXX Reserve card. One of main reasons for upgrade is that Citi XXXX Reserve card advertises 2 weekend night certificates good at select hotel and resorts within the XXXX portfolio after you make {$2500.00} in purchases within 4 months of account opening, on top of the annual benefit of earning 1 weekend stay after spending {$10000.00} in a calendar year. Since I already own the signature card, I was advised to call their customer service line to upgrade directly and apply through them to avoid getting assigned a new credit card number.
<P/>
I easily met my minimum {$2500.00} spent within the first 4 months but never received my 2 weekend night certificates. I called Citi numerous time ( at least 5 times in my recollection ), each time on the phone they claimed to understand my situation, proclaimed that I did indeed earned the promotional requirement. They even noted it on my account log. However, I subsequently receive a paper statement saying that Im not eligible for the weekend vouchers, which made no sense. I called XXXX XXXX as well regarding the situation but they said that Citi is solely responsible of letting XXXX know that I qualified for the rewards and promotions. I also learned that Citi had recently discontinue their credit card relationship with XXXX, and will subsequently phase out and/or discontinue all of the XXXX branded credit cards. It looks like I will not be receiving any of my benefits that Ive earned, which is deceitful and dishonest. Second Complaint : Ive also met the yearly benefit of 1 weekend night certificate. Through numerous conversations with Citi Card it was confirmed that I did meet the criteria to earn the reward. However, there are no steps or directions as to how to claim the 1 weekend night certificate. I was told that an email was sent out regarding the reward but I never received. I was constantly provided runaround between Citi Card and XXXX and my question was never resolved. In short, Im not able to claim the rewards that Ive earned. <P/>In summary, would someone please look into my account and shows that I did indeed earned the 2 weekend night certificate ( per {$2500.00} spent in first four month ) and 1 weekend night certificate ( per {$10000.00} spent in a year )? I had to spend {$95.00} on a credit card that I receive ZERO benefit!!!
<P/>
I 've been a loyal Citi credit card member for a long time, owns 3 different credit cards. Looks like Citi is not keeping customer loyalty and satisfaction their # 1 priority. <P/>Thank you.

( XXXX/XXXX/2015 ) I opened a Chase account with the promotion of {$300.00} for premier checking and {$200.00} for premier savings accounts. After receiving poor customer service and addressing concerns with the branch manager ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, XXXX ), I continued to receive poor customer service. Since this branch is most convenient for me, I decided to terminate my relationship Chase. 

On closing my checking account first on XX/XX/2015, the relationship banker ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) overdrew my account and reversed the promotion without informing me. I found out when I received notification through my cell phone application that my account was overdrawn and saw that the {$300.00} promotion was also reversed. I had to transfer funds from my savings account to prevent it from being overdrawn. She then informed me that there was a disclosure that I had signed that the account had to be opened for 6 months. After I notified her I was not aware of it, she told me I had signed for it and that she reopened my account. She proceeded to tell me that she will call corporate to tell them she accidently closed my account to get coupon reversal removed. 

After weeks of calling her, she told me she was not able to get coupon reversal removed ( XXXX/XXXX/2015 ). I questioned her about the 6 months policy and she told me it was written on the coupon. She also told me that since I 'm closing my accounts, she already informed everyone in the office my situation and that I could talk the branch manager about getting the coupon reversal removed. I informed her that I want to speak to someone who is his supervisor because he is the reason I am terminating my relationship with Chase. XXXX told me that their regional bank manager quit two weeks ago and that there is no one else I can talk to. I called the Chase corporate office and spoke to XXXX from the Escalation department and spent over 40 minutes with her to explain my situation. She told me a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX will return my call in two business days to address my concerns. I asked XXXX for a contact number for XXXX XXXX and she informed me I had to call the XXXX corporate number. I have not heard from XXXX XXXX. 

I reviewed my documents that I was given for my accounts. The relationship banker ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) returned the coupon he had used opening of the account. It is no where in the disclosure documents that I had to keep this account open for a minimum of 6 months to secure this bonus or on the coupon itself. All the coupon said was that {$300.00} for signing up for direct deposit for savings and {$200.00} for depositing {$15000.00} for 90 days. 

I was never disclosed of this 6 months policy upon opening of the account or closing of the account. I have no proof of documentation of acknowledging this policy and nor were they able to provide proof that they " may '' have informed me at any point. Please see my attached documents.

Consumer complaint narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, This letter is written in response to your recent communication, dated XX/XX/year> regarding an alleged debt you claim I owe. Under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), 15 U.S.C. 1692g, I am asserting my right to request and receive full and complete validation of this debt with the accounts below in questioned. 

Creditors Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX Until this request is fully satisfied, you are hereby instructed to CEASE all collection activities, including calls, letters, credit reporting, or threats of legal action. 
Let me be clear : I am NOT acknowledging or accepting this alleged debt. I am demanding, under federal law, that you provide the following documentation for validation : 1. A copy of the original signed agreement that created the debt, bearing my signature.

2. A complete accounting statement, itemizing the alleged balance, interest, fees, and how these were calculated.

3. Proof of your legal authority to collect on this debt, including a copy of the assignment or purchase agreement from the original creditor.

4. The name and address of the original creditor, including account numbers and the date the alleged debt was incurred.

5. Verification that this debt is within the statute of limitations in my state.

6. Proof that you are licensed to collect debts in my state, including your license number and issuing authority.

If you can not provide these materials, then you must immediately cease all collection attempts and remove any negative reports made to credit bureaus, in accordance with15 U.S.C. 1692g.

Your failure to validate this debt as required, and any continued collection activity or credit reporting, constitute violations of both the FDCPA and the FCRA. As a result, if this information can not be furnished accurately, I will be requesting statutory damages in the amount of {$1000.00}, pursuant to 15 U.S.C. 1692k ( a ) ( 2 ) ( A ) of the FDCPA 15 U.S.C. 1681n and 1681 of the FCRA Unless you can provide legal and verifiable proof of this alleged debt, you must immediately : Cease all collection efforts Remove all references to this debt from any credit reporting agencies Refrain from contacting me again except to provide the validation requested This letter also serves as notice to preserve all communications and documents related to this matter for potential litigation.

You have 30 days from the date of your receipt of this letter to provide the above-requested validation. If you continue to pursue collection efforts without complying, you will be in violation of federal law. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
Travelers Checks were bought in XX/XX/XXXX from XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX. ( " Bank '' ) and American Express Company ( " AmEx '' ), relying upon promised reimbursement within 24 hours of notice of lost/stolen checks. After discovering that {$1600.00} in checks were missing on XXXX/XXXX/XXXX, XXXX different claim forms provided by AmEx were submitted : first on XXXX/XXXX/XXXX ; then on XXXX/XXXX/XXXX ; and repeatedly through XXXX/XXXX/XXXX. On XXXX/XXXX/XXXX, a third set of forms ( Travelers Cheque Refund Reimbursement Agreement and Terms & Conditions-Purchase Agreement ) sent by AmEx was returned ( by certified mail, email and fax ). There have been numerous emails, faxes, and letters, and conversations with XXXX AmEx and XXXX Bank agents. See Attachment 1 Chronology ( summary of contacts ) and Attachments XXXX-XXXX ( copies of all correspondence ). 

I was advised the checks had not been and likely could not be cashed as they are on high alert. AmEx supervisor XXXX ( office in XXXX ) stated in conversation memorialized in my cover letter dated XXXX/XXXX/XXXX that my claims were satisfactory and would be paid within XXXX business days after AmEx received the third set of forms. On XXXX/XXXX/XXXX AmEx received those forms, modified as discussed with XXXX ; but they still have not reimbursed me. 

Instead, they engaged in a deliberate continuing, repetitive pattern of unfair, deceptive, abusive and discriminatory practices, which seemed designed to delay and defraud. Bank agents reported that many customers had similar problems with AmEx. And, my experience is similar to complaints on the internet of many buyers of AmEx services ( such as Travelers Checks, Bluebird, Serve, XXXX, XXXX or XXXX ), all seemingly supervised by the same agents/offices. See, eg. : internet reports in Attachment XXXX. 

On XXXX/XXXX/XXXX, XXXX 's subordinate, XXXX, telephoned to discuss the Reimbursement Agreement and Purchase Agreement. I had difficulty understanding his accent and asked him to send an email/letter specifying any problems, and precise directions for remedy. No response was received. 

On XXXX/XXXX/XXXX, I asked Bank to reimburse me, as promised in XX/XX/XXXX. I provided copies of the attached documents. I was charged interest on a recent credit card statement of XXXX which was onerous given that I had sent XXXX checks in the same envelope for the entire balance minus XXXX. After much repetition of their policy of charging interest on the average purchase balance I finally got the supervisor to tell me how that average purchase balance was calculated in my case. They applied the check of XXXX and for whatever reason, tho they posted having received the XXXX check as well on the same day, it was not included in the adjustment to my balance owing. Meaning, they calculated interest on a liability they did not have as they calculated it as tho the XXXX had never been received, tho it is clearly posted. They immediately credited me back the interest, but I was upset for all the people less fortunate than I who could have been unfairly charged, or do get unfairly charged all the time. Isn't it fraud to charge interest on a liability they did not have?
How could they do this? The resolution is not my being credited----they did that. Resolution for me is having someone explain to me how this is not fraud. And who and how and what gets to decide to credit the XXXX and not the $XXXX----if they were only going to credit one check before calculating interest, is it legal to only credit the check with the smallest amount paid in? What's up with that? The whole thing screams of fraud to me and made me sick to my stomach. Again, I am blessed and have the resources to pay in full and get "credited" when something snarky is done as I am a good customer, but what about all the others who are not so blessed, nor persistent, or just plain careful when they read their statement?

Consumer complaint narrative:
Happy Holidays ; Please remove this unknown collection account from all the major credit bureaus for failure to provide me the required documents as requested per the laws under FDCPA. A Months ago, I initiated a dispute with the MidlandMCM directly asking for all the documents and disclosure so I can validate the accuracy of this alleged debt, but they failed to provide me ALL the required documents. But after reading several cases online, where the company was sued for a similar situation, and specifically a case of " State of Texas vs. Midland Funding and their other entities and group '', where the plaintiff asked for documents and they failed to provide them. The AG outlined what the plaintiff was asking for and their nature of business. Please see detail below : 9.3 The electronic portfolios of these charged off accounts acquired by Defendants contain limited categories of information about the debt. Typically, Defendants do not acquire the underlying credit agreements, payment history, or statements ( or " media '' ) but retain the right to purchase the documentation from the original creditor at a later date. 9.4 Defendants ' collection activities prefer " efficiency '' and profits over compliance with Texas debt collection laws. Defendants chum out millions of collection letters and millions of telephone calls from its call centers in Arizona, Minnesota and XXXX, often using incomplete and/or inaccurate electronic information purchased from original creditors. Consumers complain Defendants have targeted the wrong person for collection or are attempting to collect debts that have been fully or partially paid or settled. Consumers also complain that Defendants pursue them for collection of old debts for which they do not have any records and are beyond the statute of limitations and credit reporting periods. Defendants ' form collection letters contain very little information about the debt, no supporting documentation, and no proof that ownership of the debt has been transferred to Defendants. When a citizen contacts Defendants to dispute a debt or the amount of the balance owed, or to ask for additional information, little or nothing is done to investigate or verify the legitimacy of the debt. 
9.5 Consumers complain that when they tell Defendants they do not owe the debt Defendants refuse to provide documentation to verify the debt and turn the tables on the consumer to prove the debt is not owed or not owed in the amount alleged. Consumers complain that this practice has resulted in loss of credit rating, inability to refinance their homes, and even loss of job opportunities, in addition to the aggravation of being harassed by a debt collector who refuses to verify the debt. Some consumers pay Defendants just to avoid a false report to the credit reporting agencies or avoid the harassment of a lawsuit. The XXXX XXXX XXXX reports that over 15,000 consumers have filed complaints regarding Defendants ' collection practices.

Consumer complaint narrative:
1. At this point, I do NOT want an account with Bank of America ( BofA ). I don't need anyone interceding on my behalf -- I have established my U.S. financial dealings elsewhere. Furthermore, I do not understand why personnel at BofA are so stupid as to send me updated information on accounts THEY closed. What I DO want is for BofA to be officially reprimanded. If you can not ensure BofA remunerates the monies it cost me, I hope someone can sock them with a hefty fine for unprofessionalism ( I don't have the background, time nor inclination to research the legalities of their idiocy ). 

2. I had an account for 39 years, I was a preferred rewards customer with no problems ; had my retirement and social security payments automatically deposited into my checking account. In addition to checking, I had a VISA card ( always paid the balance in full ) and two addresses I used Bill Pay for : the IRS and to transfer monies to my husband 's account in VA. 

3. In XX/XX/XXXX, problems began -- I have attached a table detailing the back and forth from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. In addition to the time, headaches and anguish on my part, BofA cost me money -- spent trying to desperately keep the account I'd had for so long : Detailed Costs : XX/XX/XXXX Roundtrip of 274 miles to obtain U.S. notary on BofA form XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Postage ( Letter to BofA Corporate Headquarters w/copies ) XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Withdrawal Fee ( BofA negated a COVERED Bill Pay Payment ) {$30.00} XX/XX/XXXX Postage ( Beneficiary Addendum & Signature Card Sent ) XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Roundtrip of 170 miles to deposit check at U.S. financial institution XXXX I also paid my taxes late this year ( hard to pay taxes when your account is frozen and your Bill Pay set up is gone ), but have not yet heard from the IRS what THAT is going to cost me. I lost my " early renewal '' rebate on my XXXX program and have to replace all my saved credit card information at numerous sites. 

4. I have no intention of fighting the array of lawyers BofA can call upon -- I assume that if the " Customer Service '' I endured is an example, than BofA must be, necessarily, well set up in this area. I now have an account at a responsible U.S. financial institution -- with email, phone and personal contacts -- my retirement and social security are now automatically deposited there. To this date I have no idea why my BofA account was closed, however there should be some regulation governing how BofA " handled '' this mess. Consumer complaint narrative:
It has come to my knowledge and attention that the improper furnishing of my nonpublic personal information without my consent is a violation of Congresss Privacy Obligation Policy, pursuant to 15 USC 6801 ( a ). 

XXXX  and XXXX  are consumer reporting agencies that must protect any and all private information about myself, the consumer, which requires written consent or instruction prior to disclosure to other third parties. Their failure to follow these steps is a violation of law pursuant to 15 USC 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ). XXXX  and XXXX  do not have any instructions from me to furnish this information to third parties, and they certainly do not have my consent to do so. Further, any and all statements to XXXX  and XXXX, verbal, written or otherwise, which : ( a ) explicitly affirm consent ; or ( b ) can be construed to imply consent for the furnishing of my nonpublic personal information is hereby REVOKED and REJECTED. I reserve and exercise my continuing right as a consumer to opt out of disclosure pursuant to 12 CFR 1016.7 ( h ) The financial institution in dispute never informed me of my rights involving the privacy of my personal information, nor have they informed me of my right to exercise nondisclosure of such information, pursuant to 15 USC 6802 ( b ) ( 1 ) ( C ). Not to mention the report itself contains information prohibited by law to be made public without proper authorization. Said report is an adverse item of information, which is not a record of a conviction of crimes antedated by more than seven years, and it is without exemption. This is a clear violation of law pursuant to 15 USC 1681c ( a ) ( 5 ). 

It is expected of all consumer reporting agencies to abide by the compliance procedures and limit the furnishing of consumer reports to the purposes permitted by law and for no other circumstance pursuant to 15 USC 1681e ( a ). Further, it is expected of all financial institutions to fulfill their duty in providing accurate information and refrain from furnishing any information relating to a consumer when they know or have reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate.

Failing to validate and investigate the information being reported on my report is a breach of my trust and FCRA Rules. I am submitting this complaint due to the lack of investigation into my original dispute for hard inquiries that were not authorized. This is an unfair practice by a credit reporting agency with no regard to how this immature practice affect the credit worthiness of a consumer.

Subject : Request for Validation of Debt - XXXX XXXX ( Original Creditor : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) XXXX. 

I am writing in response to an alleged debt. I am requesting validation of this debt as per my rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), 15 USC 1692g. I dispute the validity of this debt and am immediately requesting that you provide the following information : 1. The original contract binding me to this so-called debt. 2. The PAYMENT HISTORY with the original creditor and balance. 3. Copy of PROOF OF DEBT ASSIGNMENT. 4. Proof that I legally owe this debt to YOUR agency. 5. Proof that you are licensed and able to collect on this debt in my state. 6. The original PURCHASE AGREEMENT. This inaccurate information needs to be re-investigated immediately by both the Credit reporting agencies and the furnishers of this information that is appearing on my consumer report. NOTE : As you can see within the screenshots that I have attached, Equifax removed this account 45 days ago. Please complete this file. Pursuant to 15 USC 1681e ( b ), A credit reporting agency must maintain accurate records at all times within the files of consumers. Pursuant to 15 USC 1681i, please perform an internal re-investigation on the validity of this account in question that is showing up on your consumer report under my name and notify me of your results within 30 days of your receipt of this letter and update my credit report accordingly to your findings. If you are unable to provide proof of my requests, then the erroneous information MUST be DELETED within 30 days of your receipt of this letter from my credit report as this erroneous account is very misleading and not mine. This account is causing me great mental anguish and I am unable to obtain a mortgage at this present time due to this inaccurate information on my credit report. Debt Collectors and Debt Buyers must furnish 100 % accurate information on consumers at all times. 15 USC 168s-2 Responsibilities of Furnishers of Information to Consumer Reporting Agencies ( a ) DUTY OF FURNISHERS OF INFORMATION TO PROVIDE ACCURATE INFORMATION ( 1 ) PROHIBITION ( A ) Reporting information with actual knowledge of errors A person shall not furnish any information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if the person knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate. ( B ) Reporting information after notice and confirmation of errors a person shall not furnish information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if ( i ) the person has been notified by the consumer, at the address specified by the person for such notices, that specific information is inaccurate; and ( ii ) the information is, in fact, inaccurate. Please note that until the validity of this debt is verified, I demand that you CEASE all collection activities and CEASE REPORTING TO CREDIT BUREAUS IMMEDIATELY until complete and verified. Also, it is the duties of credit reporting agencies to uphold their grave responsibilities to supply 100 % accurate information ONLY on all consumer credit reports as the banking system itself hinges on accurate information. DELETE THIS ERRONEOUS INFORMATION from ALL credit bureaus immediately if the information can not be verified/validated. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. If this erroneous account keeps coming back as verified I will have no choice but to seek legal counsel and sue pursuant to 15 USC 1681n and 15 USC 1681o. I will seek the maximum allowed under the FCRA.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am not sure if I am in the right category .... But here is what happened. 

I. Complaint 1 : Opening an Unauthorized PayPal Cash Plus Account, which Generated an Over {$3000.00} balance in the negative I had a PayPal account since XXXX. I was never aware that a PayPal Cash Plus account was opened for me by PayPal. PayPal can not tell me when the account was opened exactly and gives totally contradictory and conflicting information. One representative writes that the PayPal Cash Plus account was opened sometimes when I accepted a payment an kept it in PayPal Balance. Another PayPal representative writes that it was just a " re-branding '' and no new account was opened ... 

So it's confusing how and when the account was opened. 

I contacted PayPal again. I was told the account was opened for me without any affirmative actions taken by me and it was opened for me ( re-branded by existing account for me ) due to Federal Regulations, which required the change. Allegedly PayPal sent me an email, sometimes between XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX, XXXX informing me about the change, which " officially went into affect on XX/XX/XXXX ''. 

PayPal can not send me a copy of the email, any application, confirmation of acceptance, or contract regarding the terms and condition of my alleged PayPal Cash Plus Account, yet PayPal insist that the PayPal Cash Plus Account terms and conditions are binding on me. 

PayPal stated " you did not have to apply. It was a rebranding based on if we had confirmed your identity already or not. It didn't change anything about your account beyond how we speak to your balance. It is now referred to as a Cash Plus Balance. Beyond that one change, nothing was changed. '' " It isn't a brand new account. Think of it like a store that goes through a remodel. Fresh coat of paint and it's like a new store ''. 

Yet another representative stated " PayPal Cash accounts are for customers who wish to keep money in PayPal [ so it's not rebranding the already existing PayPal account ]. Customers using PayPal Cash accounts will have access to basic balance functionality, including the ability to shop with balance, send money to friends and family using balance, receive money into balance, and set up a Money Pool. PayPal Cash Plus accounts are eligible for additional features like the PayPal Cash Mastercard, receiving paychecks via direct deposit, and setting organization tools like Goals. This was a change that happened to all PayPal accounts as there was a new regulations set by the government that we ill need these type of accounts to maintain a balance. '' Another PayPal rep said " The PayPal Cash account was created when a payment was received and the money was accepted and the option to keep the money in PayPal was chosen instead of transferring it a bank '' ( PayPal can not give me a date when did this allegedly happen ). 

On PayPal 's own website, PayPal states " Personal PayPal account holders also have the ability to get a PayPal Cash Plus account. You can get a PayPal Cash Plus account by applying for or enrolling in any of the features or products below : *Get the PayPal Cash Card Debit Mastercard ( or the PayPal Business Debit Mastercard ) ; *Set up direct deposit to  automatically add pay checks to balance ; *Set up Goals to manage money being held '' So it's neither rebranding, nor something you can accidentally create by keeping a balance on you PayPal account, but something you have to apply for by any of the three listed acts ... 

Yet, PayPal insist : " I have repeatedly informed you that I can not re-send an email from over a year ago. Your Cash Plus account was not " created '', it was changed just given a new name based on the level of identity confirmation that you had completed ''. 

I am confused if I should be guided by PayPal 's statement to the public posted on PayPal 's website, but the facts are the followings none the less. I, admittedly, have never enrolled or applied for PayPal Cash Plus account. I, admittedly, do not have a PayPal Cash Card Debit Mastercard. I, admittedly, do not have a direct deposit to automatically add pay checks to balance. I, admittedly, did not set up Goals to manage money being held. 

Yet, I have a PayPal Cash Plus account. 

I do not have a contract, yet PayPal insist that I am bind by the user agreement of a service I have never applied for and PayPal can not even resend me the alleged email, which triggered ( in one version of PayPal ) the opening of the PayPal Cash Plus account or pinpoint the transaction ( another version of PayPal ) through which I have created the PayPal Cash Plus account. There seemed to be a consensus that I have never applied to open the PayPal Cash Plus account. 

Now, this mysteriously opened PayPal Cash Plus account has an over {$3000.00} negative balance. 

The PayPal Cash Plus account allegedly serves as a " balance '' account. To use PayPal 's rep own words " PayPal Cash accounts are for customers who wish to keep money in PayPal. Customers using PayPal Cash accounts will have access to basic balance functionality, including the ability to shop with balance, send money to friends and family using balance, receive money into balance, and set up a Money Pool ''. 

Yet, there was a bill submitted by XXXX ( I don't have an XXXX  account and PayPal is a spinoff company of XXXX ), which bill was paid by PayPal immediately, without my consent, even though there was {$0.00} balance on by PayPal account and/or PayPal Cash Plus Account and none of the backup payment sources had sufficient funds to cover XXXX 's alleged bill. None the less, PayPal approved the transaction. 

My PayPal Cash Plus account ( for which I have never applied, enrolled, etc. and was allegedly created for me for the sole purpose to keep positive balance on it which then I can use for different purposes ) has now a negative balance of over {$3000.00}. 

II. Complaint 2 : Unauthorized Collection Practices. 

When I wanted to dispute the charge, PayPal told me that " On XX/XX/XXXX XXXX  billed you for the {$3200.00} refund through a billing agreement set up with XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. '' PayPal is not a collection company for XXXX. 

XXXX has to verify the charge. 

I do not have an XXXX account, hence I can not have a " billing agreement '' with XXXX. If XXXX   can not validate the charge and I dispute the charge, PayPal should refund me the disputed amount and charge it only after validation or after a valid judgment by XXXX  against me regarding the dispute. 

Further, and once again, I allegedly had a " balance '' account with PayPal, which was " automatically '' created for by by " rebranding '' my existing PayPal personal account and could not have had a negative balance. 

PayPal should have refused the XXXX 's charge request as there was no money neither on my PayPal personal account, nor on my PayPal Cash Plus account, nor on any of the funding sources associated with my account. 

How can I have a credit account with a negative balance with PayPal when I never applied for such? 

How can I have a negative balance on a bill I dispute and never authorized? 

I don't thing that was CFPB 's requirement with what PayPal just complied with ... .. 

Moreover, before the account went into negative balance, I discovered that there was an " auto-pay '' authorization for XXXX in my PayPal account ( among with other XXXX XXXX   companies, such as XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, etc. ). I don't recall creating any of these so called " autopay '' intentionally and not sure how they are created but let 's not get into that. Anyway, as soon I discovered XXXX  listed on " autopay '' I removed XXXX. At the time there was no charge by XXXX  on my account. PayPal later claimed that the charge came before the " autopay '' removal ( even though it did not appear on my account ). 

Nonetheless, the " autopay '' assumes automatic payments of valid charges. 

This is not the case here. 

Again, PayPal is not ( or at least not supposed to be ) the collection company for XXXX. When XXXX  can not validate a charge and I dispute the charge, it should be denied until final dispute resolution between the alleged creditor and the alleged debtor. 

PayPal also argue that the charge was as a result of XXXX 's resolution in favor of buyer, but again, ( 1 ) I do not have an XXXX  account ; ( 2 ) I did not agree to XXXX 's dispute resolution procedures ; ( 3 ) I did not agree to accept XXXX 's " fact findings '' and I did not waived my Constitutional right to a jury trial in case of a civil dispute ; ( 4 ) there is no agreement between XXXX and me in any matter in any manner ; ( 5 ) I do not agree that XXXX  can bill me directly in case XXXX finds in buyer 's favor in a transaction I am not a part of ; ( 6 ) there is no billing or any other kind of agreement between XXXX and me so XXXX can not just " bill '' me ; ( 7 ) I am the sole owner of my PayPal account ; ( 8 ) there is no judgment or arbitration award against me in connection with any claimed debt I allegedly owe to XXXX  ; ( 9 ) there no judgment or arbitration award which would state the amount I allegedly owe to XXXX  ; ( 10 ) there is no levy order by a Court with competent jurisdiction, which would authorize PayPal to accept the charge as valid over my objection ; ( 11 ) there is no levy order by a Court with competent jurisdiction which would authorize PayPal to withdraw or withheld money without my authorization from any of my accounts ; ( 12 ) PayPal is my financial company, owes me fiduciary duties, not to XXXX. PayPal can not just deduct, pardon me, create an account for me in order to enable PayPal to approve an unauthorized XXXX  charge. To add insult to injury, initially I was told that I can not even dispute the charge ( later I was allowed to file a dispute which was decided in less than 24 hours in XXXX 's favor ). 

Last but not least, Pay Pal stated to at least another PayPal account holder who opened the account in XXXX ( information can be provided to CFPB upon request ) that opening PayPal Cash or PayPal Cash account is not automatic and that one has to apply for a card and that card has to be linked to the person 's PayPal account in order to open a PayPal Cash Plus account. 

I believe CFPB should investigate this matter as ( 1 ) PayPal attempts to validate its unlawful actions ( opening PayPal Cash Plus accounts to existing users without the existing user 's knowledge or agreement and then bend and twist the facts to serve PayPal purposes, including but not limited to misstate the scope and purposes of a PayPal Cash Plus balance account ) citing CFPB 's regulations as cause ; ( 2 ) PayPal is not a collection company for XXXX, thus should not act like one, especially when a debt verification is requested by PayPal 's own customer. The charge was either an unauthorized charge, thus XXXX should prove it's validity ( assuming that PayPal is my financial institution ) or the PayPal customer should at least have a right for debt verification ( assuming that PayPal is XXXX 's collection agency ). PayPal admittedly ( confirmed it in writing ) did not even contact XXXX  regarding the transaction. Neither to validate the alleged charge nor to validate the alleged debt.

Consumer complaint narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am filing this formal complaint against the credit reporting agencies and the following creditors/furnishers for reporting inaccurate, unverifiable, and false information on my credit report in direct violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ). 
The following information is being reported inaccurately and has caused severe harm to my credit reputation, financial standing, and personal life. Despite my prior disputes with the credit bureaus, these items remain unresolved and uncorrected. 
WRONG ADDRESSES REPORTED : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, CA XXXX I have never lived at or been associated with this address in any capacity. This is a false report that must be deleted immediately. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, CA XXXX I have no connection with this address whatsoever. It appears to be the result of a file-mixing or identity error, and I demand this wrong address be permanently deleted. 
INACCURATE ACCOUNT REPORTING : XXXXXXXX XXXX Account No : XXXXXXXX XXXX | High Balance : {$470000.00} | Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX | Issue : Late Payment This account is being reported as having late payments, which is completely false. I have never made a late payment on this account, and such reporting has severely damaged my credit profile. I have always maintained timely payments and good standing. Please correct the payment history to reflect accurate information. Under FCRA 1681i, this inaccurate entry must be immediately investigated and corrected. 
XXXX XXXX Account No : XXXXXXXX XXXX | High Balance : {$20000.00} | Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX | Issue : Charge-Off This account has been listed as a charge-off, but I have no knowledge or recollection of defaulting on this account. I was never provided with proper validation or notice. Reporting a charge-off without verified documentation is a serious violation of the FCRA and FDCPA. I demand that this account be deleted entirely from my credit report unless complete verification is provided. 
XXXX XXXX Account No : XXXXXXXX XXXX | High Balance : {$5000.00} | Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX | Issue : Charge-Off This charge-off entry is false and unverifiable. I have never defaulted on any XXXX account and have not received any legitimate verification proving this claim. This violates my rights under FCRA 609 and 611. I demand that this account be deleted completely or verified with documented proof that meets FCRA standards. 
Summary of Violations : Reporting false personal addresses. 
Reporting inaccurate and unverifiable account information. 
Failing to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation as required by FCRA 611 ( a ). 
Continuing to report false data after notification of dispute, violating FCRA 1681s-2 ( b ). 
Failing to provide validation or proof of debt as required under the FDCPA. 
Requested Resolution : I am requesting the CFPBs assistance in ensuring the credit reporting agencies and data furnishers : 1. Immediately delete or correct the inaccurate account information. 
2. Remove all false addresses from my credit file. 
3. Provide written confirmation and verification of any data they choose to retain. 
4. Ensure that all future reporting complies strictly with FCRA and FDCPA requirements. 
These false reports have caused significant damage to my creditworthiness, resulting in denials of financial opportunities and emotional distress. I trust that the CFPB will take appropriate steps to hold these companies accountable and ensure full compliance with federal consumer protection laws.

Consumer complaint narrative: Even after my initial complaint filing, I am still being wrongfully treated by Wells Fargo. For the past several months they have nothing but to make me jump through hoops, and even with my initial complain filing they still asked for the same documents over and over again and what 's the point of my relationship manager. I 've only spoken with XXXX XXXX a handful of times, and do n't recall anytime where he actually answered the phone, so I had to speak with several other people who, and again, as always had a different version of events, told me different things are needed, and gave me different advice. It 's like they are only going through the motions, and after months and months of more run-a-round, they declined my request for mortgage assistance because they claim my house was vacant!? This is 100 % false and impossible because at the time there was a Tenant in the property who was paying {$4000.00} per month. No one pays {$4000.00} per month and not live in a property. Wells Fargo knew this because they received copies of the rent check several times ... because they asked for it several times. This unfair denial caused the Tenant to become unformfotable and he vacated the property, causing me to move back into the property as my primary residence, and as required I inform Wells Fargo of any change of circumstances, ... and they did nothing but deny me! Are n't they supposed to work with me? Additionally, in the denial my income was calculated wrong. I brought both of these issues up to XXXX XXXX, who did n't care one bit. I was provided a 20 day appeal period and I appealed their decision and it was denied the same day! and when I ask why?, they will not provide me with a reason. XXXX XXXX just told me that it would take 5 days to close out the file and to re-applyI waited 5 days and reapplied. But the file was n't still closed internally. Apparenlty someone else needed to push a button to close the file, and it was n't until around XX/XX/2016 that someone finally pushed the button. My file was routed to the Wells Fargo reentry team, I was put on hold for about 20 minutes and when she came back, I was asked to send in items to prove my occupancy, and I did just that, and again ... it took them less than a day to deny me. This time I 'm denied because my loan is almost 3 years delinquent. Since when was this a provision of HAMP?, and it seems rather convenient that days before the HAMP deadline that Wells Fargo comes up with yet another excuse to give me the run-a-round. Clearly, they are just taking advantage of the expiring HAMP program to stick it to the homeowner once again!

Consumer complaint narrative:
On or around XX/XX/year>, I discovered a XXXX XXXX XXXX account inaccurately reported on my TransUnion and Equifax credit file with a Pay Status of Charged Off/Collection, indicated as Monthly for 36 months, and a Date Closed and Last Payment Date of XX/XX/year>. I immediately recognized this as fraudulent because I never opened, authorized, or benefited from such an account. This constitutes both a breach of privacysince my information was used without my consentand an inaccuracy under the FCRA. 

Despite having provided a formal identity theft report ( pursuant to FCRA 605B, 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ), the creditor has submitted false or fabricated documentation to verify this fraudulent account. This action is a direct violation of FCRA 623 ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ), which obligates data furnishers to report only accurate and verifiable information. By knowingly providing false verification, the creditor continues to misrepresent the legitimacy of an identity-theft account and must immediately delete or correct the erroneous tradeline.

I did not authorize, open, or benefit from this account. 

It appears that my personal information was used without my consent, constituting a breach of privacy under 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( FCRA 604 ), which delineates permissible purposes for obtaining and reporting credit information. Upon learning of this fraudulent account, I promptly filed an identity theft report and notified both TransUnion/Equifax and the furnisher, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

Violation of the FCRA Failure to Perform a Reasonable Reinvestigation ( FCRA 611 ; 15 U.S.C. 1681i ) TransUnion and Equifax are required to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation of disputed information. Despite my formal dispute, TransUnion continued reporting this charge-off/collection without confirming that the account actually belongs to me. This violates 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 1 ), which mandates a thorough and timely investigation of disputed items.

Failure to Block Fraudulent Information ( FCRA 605B ; 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ) Under FCRA 605B, once a consumer provides an identity theft report, the consumer reporting agency must block the fraudulent information within four business days. I supplied the necessary documentation, yet TransUnion has not removed or blocked the XXXX XXXX XXXX account in the legally mandated timeframe. 

Breach of Privacy ( FCRA 604 ; 15 U.S.C. 1681b ) Only specific permissible purposes exist for accessing and reporting a consumers credit information. Reporting an account obtained through identity theft is a clear violation of these permissible purposes. TransUnions continued reporting of this fraudulent account constitutes an ongoing breach of privacy under 15 U.S.C. 1681b.

Reporting Unverified Information from the Furnisher ( FCRA 623 ; 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) ) XXXX XXXX XXXX, as a furnisher of data, must investigate disputed information and correct or delete any information it can not verify. By confirming an account that does not belong to me, the furnisher has violated 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ), and TransUnion, by extension, is failing to ensure the accuracy of reported information under FCRA 611 . 
Violation of the FDCPA Failure to Validate Debt ( FDCPA XXXX ( b ) ; XXXX U.S.C. XXXX ( b ) ) Even though this violation primarily applies to debt XXXX, TransUnion relies on data from the furnisher. If a consumer disputes a debt in writing, the collector must validate it. The presence of unvalidated or fraudulent debt on my credit report underscores that TransUnion is receiving and reporting improperly validated information. 
False or Misleading Representations ( FDCPA 807 ; 15 U.S.C. 1692e ) By continuing to report a false debt, XXXX XXXX XXXX and any acting debt collector are effectively engaging in misleading collection activities. While the FDCPA holds collectors accountable, TransUnionby relying on those misrepresentationscontributes to the dissemination of inaccurate information. 

False and Fraudulent Verification by the Creditor Despite receiving my identity theft report and related documentation, XXXX XXXX XXXX falsely verified this account as accurate. This is especially egregious given the obligations under FCRA 623 ( b ), which requires creditors to thoroughly investigate disputes and update or remove erroneous information. By reporting this fraudulent account as verified, XXXX XXXX XXXX violates the statute, and TransUnions continued reliance on this false verification deepens the FCRA breach.

Consumer complaint narrative: As mentioned in the initial dispute, according to the law you are required by law ( 12 CFR Part 1016.4 ( a ) ( 2 ) to provide a clear and conspicuous notice that accurately reflects your privacy policies and practices to consumers before you disclose any nonpublic personal information about the consumer to any nonaffiliated third party, if you make such a disclosure other than as authorized by 1016.14 and 1016.15 of this part. 12 CFR Part 1016.7 indicates you are required to provide an opt out notice under 1016.10 ( a ), you must provide a clear and conspicuous notice to each of your consumers that accurately explains the right to opt out under that section. The notice must state : ( i ) That you disclose or reserve the right to disclose nonpublic personal information about your consumer to a nonaffiliated third party ; ( ii ) That the consumer has the right to opt out of that disclosure; and ( iii ) A reasonable means by which the consumer may exercise the opt out right. 12 CFR Part 1016.7 ( g ) also states that you must comply with a consumer 's opt out direction as soon as reasonably practicable after you receive it. Specifically : FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) : Mandates that credit reporting agencies must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation if a consumer disputes the completeness or accuracy of any item of information contained in their file.

FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) : Requires furnishers of information to ensure the accuracy of the data they provide to credit reporting agencies.

Consumer Financial Protection Bureau The continued reporting of this repossession, despite full payment, constitutes inaccurate and incomplete information, violating my rights under the FCRA.

Requested Action : I request that Transunion, Equifax, and Experian take the following actions : Conduct a Reinvestigation : Initiate a thorough reinvestigation into the accuracy of the repossession entry associated with the account detailed above. 
Remove and block the account : Based on the findings of the reinvestigation, promptly update the account status to reflect its paid status or remove the repossession entry entirely from my credit report. 
Provide Verification Documentation : Supply all documentation and information used during the reinvestigation to verify the accuracy of the disputed information.

Enclosures : A copy of my credit report with the disputed repossession entry highlighted.

Proof of payment for the account in question.

Any additional supporting documentation relevant to this dispute.

Please address this matter promptly and provide a written response detailing the outcome of your reinvestigation within 30 days, as stipulated by the FCRA.

Thank you for your attention to this important matter. I look forward to your timely resolution of this issue to ensure the accuracy and integrity of my credit report.

We received a call from Chase Bank on our HELOC. They said that we were short a payment. The statement was dated XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, and the minimum payment line said {$97.00}. The coupon clearly states that the payment of {$97.00} is due on XX/XX/XXXX. We made the payment, which was cashed and posted in their system on XX/XX/XXXX. 
They claim that that payment was for the month of XX/XX/XXXX, and that the XX/XX/XXXX payment of {$100.00} was not received. 
We have statement covering the dates XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, which shows a minimum payment of {$100.00}, and the coupon shows a due date of XX/XX/XXXX. Our bank statement shows that the check was cashed, and their statement with the XX/XX/XXXX due date clearly shows that they received the payment on XX/XX/XXXX. 
Basically, I have sequential statements, going back to at least XX/XX/XXXX, showing the exact amounts requested. They also emailed me copies that include the payment vouchers, and all of them match the payments we have made. 

They are claiming that the payment due XX/XX/XXXX is the XX/XX/XXXX payment, and the XXXX that we paid is a short payment for XX/XX/XXXX, and that we owe another $ XXXX. They did this to us back in XX/XX/XXXX as well, getting a whole extra payment out of us. 

I tried to speak to their Customer Service. The first rep kept trying to convince us, but we have documented proof, including their own statements. The follow up research person, when first contacting us, could not understand why THEIR department was incorrect. She called us back this morning with the same explanation as the first, and then became condecending. They claimed that because we had filed for a bankruptcy almost ten years ago, that the statements were incorrect, and that we had to look at the COUPON amount. Well, I did that, and the payments made all matched the coupons.

Again, we have proof that everything had been paid.

If they are making us pay extra for these " mistakes, '' What if they are doing this to ALL of their customers? System clitch? How much extra revenue are they making from this?

Consumer complaint narrative:
This formal complaint concerns unauthorized inquiries appearing on my credit report, made without my consent and lacking a permissible purpose under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). These inquiries are negatively impacting my credit profile and do not meet the FCRAs requirements for accessing a consumers credit report without express authorization. 
Details of Disputed Inquiries : The following inquiries are disputed, as verified by the creditors, who confirmed they were conducted without my knowledge or authorization : Creditor Name : XXXX E Q U I F A X Date of Inquiry : XX/XX/XXXX Creditor Name : XXXX  Date of Inquiry : XX/XX/XXXX Creditor Name : XXXX  Date of Inquiry : XX/XX/XXXX Creditor Name : XXXX CARD Date of Inquiry : XX/XX/XXXX Creditor Name : XXXX Date of Inquiry : XX/XX/XXXX Legal Basis for Removal : Under the FCRA, specifically Section 604 ( 15 U.S.C. 1681b ), access to a credit report is only permissible when explicit consumer consent or a clearly permissible purpose exists. The listed inquiries do not meet these legal requirements. Additionally, under Section 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ), any unverifiable information must be promptly removed from the credit report. 
I have attempted to resolve this matter directly with the credit bureau by submitting a formal dispute letter on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX. However, I have not received an adequate response or the removal of the unauthorized inquiries, which I believe constitutes noncompliance with FCRA provisions. 
Supporting Case Law for Removal : Legal precedents reinforce the need for removal of unauthorized inquiries in the absence of a permissible purpose. In XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ), the XXXX XXXX ruled that accessing a credit report without explicit consumer consent or a legitimate purpose constitutes a violation of the FCRA. Furthermore, XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ) reaffirms that credit bureaus are obligated to remove all inaccurate or unverifiable information.

Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX - XXXX XXXX XXXX - XXXX I am writing to formally dispute multiple late payments reported on my credit file, which are inaccurate due to unauthorized access and account tampering involving my personal information. 
Over the past one to two years, I experienced a pattern of unexpected missed payments across several of my credit accounts. During this period, I was in a relationship where the individual had access to my personal laptop and login credentials. After the relationship ended, I discovered that this person had deliberately tampered with my accounts disabling autopay, removing saved payment methods, and altering my email and notification settings without my knowledge or consent. 
The individual later admitted to me directly over the phone that they accessed my accounts maliciously and interfered with my financial information. These actions were taken without my authorization and directly caused the missed payments on accounts that were otherwise in good standing. 
Since uncovering this, I have : Secured all of my financial accounts. 
Implemented stronger security measures to prevent future incidents. Under federal law, these facts constitute unauthorized use of my personal information and account takeover, qualifying as identity theft under the Federal Trade Commissions definition of account takeover fraud. Accordingly, I have the right to file an Identity Theft Report with the Federal Trade Commission and law enforcement.

In exercising my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and related statutes, I am demanding the immediate removal of these late payments from my credit report : 15 U.S. Code 1681s2 : Requires furnishers to report only accurate, verif
iable information and correct inaccuracies upon notice.

15 U.S. Code 1681i : Requires consumer reporting agencies to investigate disputes and delete unverifiable or inaccurate information within 30 days.

15 U.S. Code 1681c-2 : Provides for blocking information resulting from identity theft or fraudulent use of personal identifying information.

15 U.S. Code 45 ( FTC Act ) : Prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices, which includes the reporting of inaccurate consumer data.

E-SIGN Act ( 15 U.S. Code Chapter 96 ) : Requires proper consent and authentication for any electronic authorizations or changes to account settings. 
Furthermore, it would be impossible for the furnisher to provide documented proof that I personally made the changes that led to these missed payments, as no such evidence exists. In the absence of verifiable documentation directly attributing these actions to me, federal law requires the deletion of these negative items.

To resolve this matter efficiently, you may elect to remove or forgive these late payments as a final resolution. Doing so will fully release you from any potential liability associated with this dispute, while not constituting an admission of fault or acknowledgment of any inaccurate data reporting. This approach provides a clear, lawful resolution and avoids the need for further escalation. 
Please be advised that I am not requesting, nor do I authorize, the closure or restriction of my accounts. I am only requesting the removal of the inaccurately reported late payments. My accounts should remain open and in good standing following the resolution of this dispute. Please note, if this matter is not resolved promptly, I reserve the right to submit an Identity Theft Report to the FTC, and to escalate my complaint to regulatory bodies including the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) and the Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ). 
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I expect written confirmation of the outcome of your investigation within the time period required by law.

Issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
Sub-issue: Their investigation did not fix an error on your report
Consumer complaint narrative:
This is my XXXX formal complaint to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau regarding the mishandling of unauthorized transactions, delayed support, and inaccurate credit reporting by Boeing Employees Credit Union ( BECU ). My original complaint, CFPB Case XXXX XXXX, failed to yield a satisfactory resolution, despite presenting clear evidence of unauthorized use and my proactive efforts to resolve the issue. The institutions most recent response dated XX/XX/XXXX, neither acknowledges the reported fraud nor addresses the harmful and ongoing consequences of inaccurate credit reporting. 

Background Context : Trust Exploited, Fraudulent Use In late XXXX through early XXXX, I experienced severe financial hardship. During this time, I enlisted the help of a trusted individual to assist with errands and transportation. Unbeknownst to me, this individual took advantage of my vulnerable position and used my XXXX credit card to make unauthorized purchases. The most significant of these transactions occurred in Wyoming, where my credit card was usedwithout my permissionto pay for the rebuilding of a semi-truck engine, an expense clearly unrelated to my financial profile or daily needs. 

At no point did I provide verbal or written authorization for this charge. I was not physically present in XXXX at the time of the transaction, nor do I own or operate a semi-truck. The individual involved did not have legal permission to use my financial instruments, and this transaction constitutes fraudulent activity. 

Initial Response by XXXX : Delayed and Deficient Upon reviewing my account statements and identifying the unauthorized charge, I immediately contacted XXXX to report the fraudulent transaction and seek relief under their hardship payment arrangement policies. However, my initial attempts for assistance were dismissed or neglected. I made multiple calls in early XXXX but was only offered meaningful help after speaking to a XXXX representative named XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Pariss involvement led to a hardship arrangement being initiated, but by then, the account had already begun to accrue late fees and negative reporting due to the delay in support. This failure to act promptly reflects poor internal coordination and contradicts the fair treatment expected under Regulation XXXX and FCRA XXXX ( a ) ( XXXX ) ( A ), which requires financial institutions to ensure the accuracy and integrity of the data they furnish. 

Good Faith Payments Despite Financial and Emotional Toll Even though I was the victim of fraud and had no obligation to pay for these unauthorized charges, I took full responsibility and made good faith payments. Specifically : On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a payment that satisfied the XXXX due date. 
Shortly thereafter, I made a {$6000.00} payment, which significantly reduced the account balance. 
These payments were made not because I was liable for the charges, but because I was trying to maintain a good relationship with XXXX  and protect my credit historydespite the emotional distress, financial strain, and violation of trust I endured. 

XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Response : Inadequate and Legally Deficient XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, letter ignored key aspects of my report : It acknowledged receipt of payments but refused to consider the fraud or the nature of the unauthorized charges. 
It claimed that delinquent reporting was valid under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX, but made no mention of my report of unauthorized use or the specifics of the Wyoming charge. 
It failed to provide any evidence of a proper fraud investigation, which they are obligated to conduct under FCRA XXXX and XXXX ( b ) once a dispute has been submitted. 
No documentation or receipts were sent confirming they reviewed the XXXX charge or contacted the vendor involved. 

Legal Violations and Procedural Gaps FCRA XXXX ( a ) requires a furnisher to perform a reasonable reinvestigation upon disputeBECU failed to do this. 
FCRA XXXX ( b ) mandates that if fraud is alleged and a dispute is submitted via a consumer reporting agency, the furnisher ( BECU ) must investigate and report the findings. No such investigation occurred here. 
Under Regulation XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), XXXX had a duty to correct or delete incomplete or inaccurate informationbut instead they continued reporting delinquency tied to a fraudulent transaction. 
By failing to act upon the XXXX report and now ignoring the second, XXXX is showing a pattern of willful noncompliance, which XXXX constitute negligence or willful violation, opening the door to civil liability under FCRA XXXX and XXXX. 

Summary of Impact A fraudulent charge made in a different state, by someone who exploited my trust, continues to weigh on my credit report. 
I am a documented hardship customer who made multiple good faith payments despite not being liable for the original charge. 
I reported the charge XXXX but XXXX has never acknowledged it as fraud or conducted an appropriate investigation. 
This has caused undue emotional distress, credit score damage, and limited my access to financial products that I would otherwise qualify for.

Product: Checking or savings account
Issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
Consumer complaint narrative:
Because of my consistently low bank balance and foreign sounding name, I have been discriminated when the bank uses banks " security '' policies, rules and regulations to harass, threaten and fine me into dismissal if I don't pay unsolicited or unpublicized fees and fines simply because I don't purchase the kinds of products that earn the bank more revenue. 

Although I have registered many complaints about how after years of paying excessive fines/fees that were never advertised or disclosed when I opened the accounts, the customer service is poor and sometimes illegal ( because of various data breaches and poor technology services that keep me from accessing my money in a convenient manner ), I have put up with this because of the administrative hassle of changing banks, and as a result have been a loyal and a legally abiding customer for years. 

Yet, because one client gave me one fraudulent check, I was being threatened in a hostile tone by one customer service with the prospect of being discharged from the bank for being deemed " at risk ''. I have been talked down to in unprofessional, informal manner and gaslighted and forced to take the blame for " allowing '' myself to receive a bad check even though I was the victim. 

I am a business owner and often have to assume risk that some checks might be bad. It doesn't mean that I should be treated like a common criminal like this company treated me as as retaliation and harassment for passionately complaining about their illegal and profit scheming practices the day before. These threats and this harassment to discharge me is not only discriminatory, but has systematically resulted through years of non-advertised fines and fees.
