Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
Consumer complaint narrative:
XX/XX/2023. 
I was selling a couch on XXXX and a scammer wanted to Zelle me. I did not know this was a scam hype. I sent the scammer {$200.00} because I thought Zelle was updating my account to business so I could get all the funds. I googled Zelles customer service number and as soon as I called them asking what was happening, they assured me they were here to help. I wanted to know if it was a scam and the representative told me they would help me, but needed to see my screen. I downloaded the XXXX app that screen shares and I was immediately nervous and confused. The representative lead me to believe he was helping me get the full amount, the buyer on XXXX was trying to send me. I told him I dont want that money, its fake, and I want my {$200.00} I sent them given back to me. 
Then he continued to make me believe that I needed to have all these transactions on file to match some amount that I was being refunded and I yelled at him that it makes no sense but XXXX me, I did it anyway. I Zelled an unknown email with my name on it ( wasnt mine ). I also sent money through XXXX XXXX to a friend. He sent it back and then the guy made me buy bitcoin and withdrawal it! Why?! 

I called two different Zelle representatives. Both were male, non English speaking, and nice to me the whole time. They drained my account and after they couldnt touch my savings account, they told me Im out of luck and to ask my bank for help. 
I disputed all transactions through XXXX and ten days later they told me I sent the money so I wont get my money back. ( I saw the documents they sent me but they are no longer there ) The act that protects people like me says otherwise. I was a naive victim to some kind of crazy scam, that I am upset at myself for falling for. That {$1500.00} was not sent for any material or service reason. 

Update* XX/XX/2023 We called Zelle this morning and they stated there was no record of me ever calling. *

XX/XX/XXXX2025 Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, DC XXXX Subject XXXX Formal Complaint Regarding Inaccurate Credit Reporting Dear CFPB, I am writing to formally request your assistance in addressing an ongoing issue with the credit reporting agencies. Despite my repeated disputes and submissions of supporting documentation, inaccurate information continues to appear on my credit reports. These inaccuracies are significantly affecting my creditworthiness, causing harm to my financial standing and opportunities. 
I believe the credit reporting agencies have failed to properly investigate and address my disputes in accordance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). I have made every effort to correct this misinformation, yet the errors persist. The continued reporting of inaccurate information has led to financial and emotional distress, as it impacts my ability to obtain fair credit terms and other essential financial opportunities. 
I am requesting the following actions : 1. Immediate investigation into my claims by the CFPB. 
2. Removal, deletion, and blocking of all inaccurate information from my credit reports. 
3. Monetary relief for the financial and emotional harm caused by the negligent reporting practices. 
I have attached copies of my correspondence with the credit reporting agencies, as well as any supporting documents relevant to this matter. Please let me know if additional information is required to assist with your investigation. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. I trust that the CFPB will take the necessary steps to ensure that the credit reporting agencies comply with their obligations under federal law. I look forward to your prompt response and resolution. Consumer complaint narrative:
My complaint supports the below findings and is in direct violation of my rights as a PICK-A-PAYMENT mortgage scam victim. Please ref account known as XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a federal judge found Wells Fargo Bank is in breach of a XXXX nationwide mortgage loan class action settlement involving adjustable payments for home mortgages, since the bank allegedly did not properly evaluate homeowners who applied for these types of loan payments in order to avoid foreclosure. 
U.S. District Judge XXXX XXXX asked Wells Fargo to meet with the plaintiffs in order to find a new way to fix the mortgage loan class action settlement violations, which will need to include steps that will allow homeowners to reapply for loan assistance through Wells Fargo Bank. 
Judge XXXX decision is just the most recent of a long list of disputes regarding the XXXX Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement, an agreement which resolved several complaints related to the banks pick-a-payment loans, many of which were acquired by Wells Fargo during their XXXX acquisition of Wachovia Corp . 
These pick-a-payment loans allowed borrowers to initially pay less interest than was due on their mortgage ; however, the escalating payments that came later as a result of these types of mortgage loans contributed to the thousands of home foreclosures that occurred during the countrys XXXX housing crisis. For years the plaintiffs legal representatives argued Wells Fargo was in violation of the Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement agreement, in that the bank was not granting loan modifications that would have been worth {$2.00} billion to homeowners with Wells Fargo pick-a-payment mortgage loans. These modifications were an integral part of the Wells Fargo mortgage loan settlement agreement, which also stipulated the bank would pay {$50.00} million to eligible Class Members. 
However, according to the plaintiffs new Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action lawsuit, the bank was not using proper methodologies to evaluate whether or not a homeowner was at risk of imminent default, which would have qualified them for loan assistance under the previous Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement. 
The plaintiffs legal representatives in this mortgage loan class action lawsuit allege thousands of homeowners were denied the mortgage assistance due to them because Wells Fargo Bank used faulty methods to measure their financial hardships pertaining to the pick-a-payment home loans. 
In his recent ruling, Judge XXXX reprimanded both sides of this Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action lawsuit, stating that it seemed that neither side knew exactly what they agreed to more than four years ago, during the initial Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement. However, the judge agreed with the plaintiffs arguments that the bank breached the settlement agreement by using evolving and ill-defined standards when determining homeowners loan modification applications. 
The court has ordered that both Wells Fargo and the suing plaintiffs present a joint or competing proposals to correct the mortgage loan class action settlement violations to be submitted within the next two weeks.

The Wells Fargo Mortgage Loan Class Action Lawsuit is In re : Wachovia Corp. Pick-A-Payment Mortgage Marketing and Sales Practices Litigation, Case No. XXXX, in the U.S. District Court for the XXXXXXXX District of California XXXX

Below are the issues that were not addressed and/or need to be re-addressed due to their inaccuracy. 

TD XXXX XXXX in the previous complaint : 1 ) On XX/XX/XXXX, your online account was successfully enrolled in electronic statements. Once enrolled in electronic statements, physical statements are no longer mailed. Your monthly billing statements reflect all transactions, including payments, returned payments, and all fees assessed, until the cycle closing date. It is the customer 's obligation to access and review the electronic statements available online. We have no record or other evidence that you were unable to access the statements based upon an error or outage attributable to us. 

My Response to that assertion : In the attached document from TD XXXX  online portal, you can see that for the month of XX/XX/XXXX and the month of XX/XX/XXXX, it states " NO STATEMENT FOR THIS MONTH. '' As previously stated in Complaint # XXXX XXXX, " TD XXXX  failed to send a copy of the monthly statement at the onset of the relationship. As a result of that oversight and neglect, I have continually incurred fees and was spun into a cycle of confusion regarding how payments were structured, applied and what was actually owed. I had not received any statements after the purchase I made and reached out to TD XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX inquiring about when my bill was due. '' Conclusion to this assertion : TD XXXX did not make a monthly statement available to me for the months of XXXX and XXXX of XXXX. Therefore, I was unaware of when my statements generated AND when they were due. 


TD XXXX XXXX  in the previous complaint : 2 ) Our records indicate that on XX/XX/XXXX, you accessed our online account management service and added bank account ending in XXXX with routing number XXXX to your payment account enrollments. Please note, we received your minimum monthly payment made from the above-mentioned account for the XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX billing cycles. Those payments were successfully posted to your account. 

We received your payment ( s ) of {$260.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, and on XX/XX/XXXX. However, the payments were returned due to reason code XXXX, indicating no account found. As a result of the returned payments, returned payment fees totaling {$70.00} was assessed to your account. 

My response to that assertion : TD XXXXXXXX  admits that there were payments from account ending in XXXX that successfully cleared my account. However, TD XXXX  also asserts that " the payment ( s ) of {$260.00} that were made on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, were returned due to reason code XXXX, indicating no account found. '' These payments were also processed through the account ending in XXXX ; the same account that the previous payments successfully cleared from. 

Conclusion to this assertion : TD XXXX either did not process my payments or there was a system error. If the account was not found on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, the prior payments would not have successfully cleared for months ' XXXX, XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. 

TD XXXX asserts in the previous complaint : 3 ) Your minimum monthly payment of {$94.00} was due on XX/XX/XXXX. We did not receive your payment and a {$40.00} late fee was assessed to your account. Under the terms of your account, any late or other fees assessed during the billing cycle are added to your required minimum payment. Your minimum monthly payment of {$220.00} was due on XX/XX/XXXX. We received your online payment of {$94.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. However, the payment did not satisfy the minimum monthly payment requested. We have no record of any communication with customer service where you were advised to make a payment of {$94.00}. A payment which is less than the required minimum payment is considered late, unless additional payments are received that satisfy the minimum monthly payment before the due date. Thus, a {$40.00} late fee was assessed to your account. However, we have waived this fee, as a courtesy. 

My response to this assertion : TD XXXX  states that there is no record of any communication with customer service where I was advised to make a payment of {$94.00}. TD XXXX  also states that I paid the amount of {$94.00} online on XX/XX/XXXX. However, according to the monthly statement for XX/XX/XXXX, TD XXXX  lists my monthly payment as : Transaction Date : XX/XX/XXXX Posting Date : XX/XX/XXXX Location Reference # XXXX Transaction Description : AGENCY PMT. 
PLEASE NOTE : An Agency Payment signifies that the payment was made with an AGENT or through the AGENCY. 

When payments are made online through the TD XXXX portal, the payment reads : Transaction Date : XX/XX/XXXX Posting Date : XX/XX/XXXX Location Reference XXXX XXXX XXXX Transaction Description : XXXX XXXX There is a distinct difference in how the payment reads on the statement when it is processed with a representative and when it is customer initiated. Consumer complaint narrative:
REQUEST FOR FREE ANNUAL CREDIT REPORT!!! 
I believe that each consumer reporting agency has invalidated the true spirit of the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act. The Act first established XX/XX/2003 was intended to establish a plan whereas common American citizens could obtain a copy of their credit reports at least once a year free of charge. Since each credit reporting agency now has marketing divisions such as XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX   and XXXX XXXXXXXX, the automation system to obtain the free annual reports is looped into an information request to steer citizens into additional product offers that are excessive and unnecessary. Even though I may have received an approval of an updated credit report from either XXXX, XXXX XXXX or Equifax respectively, it is unfair that I need to do a mail request since I can submit my personal information through the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) portal. I  believe that since the CFPB is the sole responsible oversight party of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, its imperative that any additional solicitation of information from the credit bureaus should be blocked permanently. I along with any other citizen should receive a free credit report upon request. The credit reporting agencies are an important aspect of the national banking system to properly function, but the current aggressive marketing campaign soliciting citizens for an arbitrary FICO score has gone too far, including but not limited to XXXX   XXXX. We must return to a common ground that is fair for all consumers in the United States, and we must be able to request additional marketing on our own terms. Accordingly, I am making a request for a free credit report with confirmation number to my attached email address. Thank you.

I am writing to you regarding a violation of my privacy and confidentiality rights, protected under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 USC 1681b ( 2 ) ). Pursuant to 18 USC 8, I have identified the following accounts that have breached these legal provisions : XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXXAccount # : XXXX XX/XX/XXXXAccount # : XXXXXX/XX/XXXXAccount # : XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Account # : XXXX These accounts have accessed my credit information without my explicit authorization, which constitutes a violation of my privacy and confidentiality rights. Additionally, some of these accounts contain obsolete information that should be excluded from my credit report as stipulated in 15 USC 1681c ( a ) ( 5 ).

According to 15 USC 1681, Section 602 ( A ), I have the right to privacy. Furthermore, under 15 USC 1681, Section 604 ( A ), Section 2, a consumer reporting agency can not furnish an account without my written instructions. Also, 15 USC 1681c ( a ) ( 5 ) states that no consumer reporting agency may include any adverse item of information that is more than seven years old, other than records of convictions of crimes.

Finally, under 15 USC 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ), no person shall furnish any information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if the person knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate. Additionally, according to 15 USC 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( B ), a person shall not furnish information if ( i ) the person has been notified by the consumer that specific information is inaccurate, and ( ii ) the information is, in fact, inaccurate.

Consumer complaint narrative: I was victim of Identity Theft, somebody stole my personal information to open credit cards and get medical care. Except as otherwise provided in this section, a consumer reporting agency shall block the reporting of any information in the file of a consumer that the consumer identifies as information that resulted from an alleged identity theft, not later than 4 business days after the date of receipt by such agency of ( 1 ) appropriate proof of the identity of the consumer ; ( 2 ) a copy of an identity theft report ; ( 3 ) the identification of such information by the consumer ; and ( 4 ) a statement by the consumer that the information is not information relating to any transaction by the consumer. ( b ) Notification. A consumer reporting agency shall promptly notify the furnisher of information identified by the consumer under subsection ( a ) of this section ( 1 ) that the information may be a result of identity theft ; ( 2 ) that an identity theft report has been filed ; ( 3 ) that a block has been requested under this section ; and ( 4 ) of the effective dates of the block. ( c ) Authority to decline or rescind. ( 1 ) In general. A consumer reporting agency may decline to block, or may rescind any block, of information relating to a consumer under this section, if the consumer reporting agency reasonably determines that ( A ) the information was blocked in error or a block was requested by the consumer in error ; ( B ) the information was blocked, or a block was requested by the consumer, on the basis of a material misrepresentation of fact by the consumer relevant to the request to block ; or ( C ) the consumer obtained possession of goods, services, or money as a result of the blocked transaction or transactions. ( 2 ) Notification to consumer. If a block of information is declined or rescinded under this subsection, the affected consumer shall be notified promptly, in the same manner as consumers are notified of the reinsertion of information under section 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ) of this title. ( 3 ) Significance of block. For purposes of this subsection, if a consumer reporting agency rescinds a block, the presence of information in the file of a consumer prior to the blocking of such information is not evidence of whether the consumer knew or should have known that the consumer obtained possession of any goods, services, or money as a result of the block. ( d ) Exception for resellers. ( 1 ) No reseller file. This section shall not apply to a consumer reporting agency, if the consumer reporting agency ( A ) is a reseller ; ( B ) is not, at the time of the request of the consumer under subsection ( a ) of this section, otherwise furnishing or reselling a consumer report concerning the information identified by the consumer ; and ( C ) informs the consumer, by any means, that the consumer may report the identity theft to the Bureau to obtain consumer information regarding identity theft. ( 2 ) Reseller with file. The sole obligation of the consumer reporting agency under this section, with regard to any request of a consumer under this section, shall be to block the consumer report maintained by the consumer reporting agency from any subsequent use, if ( A ) the consumer, in accordance with the provisions of subsection ( a ) of this section, identifies, to a consumer reporting agency, information in the file of the consumer that resulted from identity theft ; and ( B ) the consumer reporting agency is a reseller of the identified information. ( 3 ) Notice. In carrying out its obligation under paragraph ( 2 ), the reseller shall promptly provide a notice to the consumer of the decision to block the file. Such notice shall contain the name, address, and telephone number of each consumer reporting agency from which the consumer information was obtained for resale. ( e ) Exception for verification companies. The provisions of this section do not apply to a check services company, acting as such, which issues authorizations for the purpose of approving or processing negotiable instruments, electronic fund transfers, or similar methods of payments, except that, beginning 4 business days after receipt of information described in paragraphs ( 1 ) through ( 3 ) of subsection ( a ) of this section, a check services company shall not report to a national consumer reporting agency described in section 1681a ( p ) of this title, any information identified in the subject identity theft report as resulting from identity theft. ( f ) Access to blocked information by law enforcement agencies. No provision of this section shall be construed as requiring a consumer reporting agency to prevent a Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency from accessing blocked information in a consumer file to which the agency could otherwise obtain access under this subchapter.

Product: Credit card or prepaid card
Consumer complaint narrative:
I was denied for a credit card and Synchrony can not explain why. I have an Ashley Furniture credit card through Synchrony Bank that I have had for a little over a year. My minimum payment every month is {$28.00} and I pay at least {$50.00}, and it is always paid early. I only have {$400.00} left on the Ashley Furniture balance so I applied for a credit card at Rooms to Go through Synchrony Bank in order to purchase new living room furniture. I received a letter on XX/XX/20 stating my credit application was denied due to " unsatisfactory payment history with Synchrony ''. I called their customer service number immediately and was advised that they have no access to my credit report and my only recourse was to write a letter to dispute the denial or call the credit bureaus. I asked if there was another department I could call to talk to someone, as I want to make sure my questions get answered and I was advised by the customer service rep that they do not have a phone # to provide me. 

I obtained all 3 of my credit reports immediately after speaking with the agent ( on XXXX ) and ALL of my reports show NO late payments on ANY account, and my credit reports show that I am an excellent risk. I take pride in maintaining a high credit score ( currently over XXXX ) and making all payments in full and earlier than the due date. Additionally, the denial letter states the reason for denial is unsatisfactory payment history WITH Synchrony - they should be able to look into my payment history and tell me what the unsatisfactory history is, because all of the credit bureaus indicate that I have paid Synchrony on time always ( which I have ). 

On XXXX, I logged into my account online and " chatted '' with an agent. The agent told me to call the application department and provided a phone #, however, the agent was also unable to tell me what my " unsatisfactory payment history '' was. The agent then transferred my chat to a manager who told me the same thing - they could not advise why the application was denied and that I needed to contact the credit bureaus. I advised the manager that the credit bureaus show excellent credit and no late payments, so I wasn't sure what I would even ask them/say to them. The manager told me that they make their decision based off of the credit bureaus and I again told him that the credit bureaus show near-perfect credit. I asked the manager who ultimately makes the final decision whether to approve or deny the application and he advised me that Synchrony does in fact make the final decision. But no one can seem to explain why my application has been denied and I am continuously directed to the credit bureaus. Synchrony made the decision to deny my application and they can not explain why.

Consumer complaint narrative:
After realizing that my credit card was lost/stolen, and noticing fraudulent transactions on my account portal, I filed disputes with Capital One against multiple fraudulent transactions on my card. My card was used to run up almost {$500.00} in purchases across multiple days, and from what I can tell based on the merchant names this was done primarily at theme park locations in Florida- I live in XXXX, OREGON and have not been to Florida in YEARS, nor was I anywhere near there at the times of these transactions, I haven't so much as left my state since XXXX. 
Capital One did NOT do their job in investigating this dispute - literally the same day XX/XX/XXXX that they acknowledged the dispute and processed a provisional credit they claimed that I " benefitted from these transactions '' and reversed the credit, closing my dispute with no evidence or explanation whatsoever of their alleged reasoning. 
I clearly DID NOT benefit from these transactions in any way, nor did I know about them or authorize them. They are NOT my purchases, and I sure as XXXX will not be paying Capital One for them. There is no chance they did an adequate job whatsoever looking into these fraudulent purchases given the timeline of their supposed " investigation '' and had they done their job they would've closed this dispute in my favor, as these are very clearly NOT MY CHARGES. 
In their letters/emails to me Capital One didn't even include all the charges I clearly laid out in my dispute, so clearly didn't adequately file/process my full dispute/claim. 
The moment I found out about these transactions- which only occurred because I saw my billing statement, I was never notified this was occurring during the time of the transactions despite having notifications set up that should've alerted me anytime there was a purchase- I locked my credit card and secured my account. I mailed Capital One documents to file the dispute - since they don't offer a customer service email and I can not call over the phone as XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for XXXX XXXX- and very clearly outlined each unauthorized transaction, merchant name, amount, and date. 
If Capital One thinks they're getting so much as a penny from me they're mistaken - I will not be making payments on purchases I did not make, and I will fight them to the end of time until these are reversed/removed from my account, and will be disputing these with all 3 credit agencies as well.

Consumer complaint narrative:
In XXXX 2015, I started using the Nationstar Mortgage website to make payments. ( XXXX ). 

When making a payment, the website will confirm if you will be charged any fees based on the payment date you select. You receive both a confirmation through the website and afterwards via email confirming the total amount that will be deducted from your bank account. 

In XXXX 2015, I selected a payment date of XXXX/XXXX/15. The online payment confirmation screen gave no notification I would be charged any fees. The email confirmation showed I would not be charged any fee. However, upon checking my bank statement, the withdrawal was {$9.00} more than what my confirmation statement showed. 
When I logged into my online Nationstar Mortgage account, a fee of {$9.00} was charged on that transaction that was not shown on my payment confirmation. 

In their online payment system, if you select certain dates the payment system shows on the confirmation screen that a charge of {$9.00} would be applied. In XXXX when I submitted my payment, I intentionally selected a date that did NOT populate the {$9.00} fee. 

Also, when scheduling my XXXX payment, there was a new notification box that popped up advising me that a fee was being waived because I am a new Nationstar member. I am clearly not a " new '' member. 

When I called Nationstar Mortgage I was advised that anytime you make a payment after 9 days, you will be charged a late fee that does not show up on the confirmation. However, the screen shot today clearly shows the fee does show up on the confirmation, and the fee always previously showed up on the confirmation as well. 

My confirmation statement from XXXX clearly confirms I would not be charged any fee. I have also frequently submitted payments to be paid on the XXXX of the month without ever incurring the fee before as proven by my transaction history. 

When I pushed the CSR for who I could submit a complaint to that was not Nationstar ( what agency regulates mortgage companies ) I was told " no one ''. I then advised the CSR to ask their supervisor and after a very long hold the CSR returned to again advise me there was no one other than Nationstar to submit the complaint to. 

I informed the CSR I found an agency to submit the complaint to, and requested a refund of the {$9.00}. I also asked for my XX/XX/XXXX draft be canceled so I could reschedule it because from what the CSR told me, even though the online website and confirmation email advised me there would be no fee, because of my date selected I would expect another {$9.00} fee at the time of withdrawal. 

I asked the {$9.00} fee for XX/XX/XXXX to be waived. CSR advised could request but could make a guarantee of refund.I requested a supervisor. After another long hold I spoke to Supervisor XXXX ( ID XXXX ). I advised I was submitting this complaint, she requested I send a copy to their complaint dept and I agreed. I advised their website has always informed me of the {$9.00} fee and I have always deliberately scheduled my payment to avoid it. I also informed her of the " fee waived '' message today. She did submit a request to waive my {$9.00} fee and I wanted it put back into the bank account they drafted it from. She called me back today to inform me they can not do that and instead will have to overnight a check. 

My concern is for consumers who live paycheck to paycheck and how this impacts them when fees are taken out above and beyond what the mortgage company confirms they will draft. I have another home loan through another mortgage company and Nationstar is the ONLY mortgage company that has informed me they can charge a fee AFTER a confirmation of payment that would change the amount drafted from a bank account. This is a dishonest practice and full disclosure of all fees AT TIME OF SCHEDULED payment should be disclosed CLEARLY.

Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX XXXX is being reported as a {$8200.00} charge-off with inconsistent XXXX XXXX fields and missing DOFD/verification. The tradeline shows charge-off/collection status while also listing conflicting dates and no valid verification. The entry remains coded Account not disputed despite repeated disputes. These practices violate FCRA 602 ( a ), 607 ( b ), 611 ( a ) and XXXX XXXX reporting rules. 
What I did ( short ) : I disputed the Capital XXXX tradeline with Equifax and TransUnion over XXXX  days ago and requested verification of the debt and XXXX XXXX field corrections. Neither bureau has supplied the required documentation or corrected the file. 
What would be a fair resolution ( REWRITTEN strong / detailed ) : Delete the XXXX XXXX {$8200.00} charge-off immediately unless XXXX XXXX provides full, contemporaneous verification that complies with the standards below. If verification is provided, then correct all XXXX XXXX fields so they are internally consistent ( accurate account status, payment rating, DOFD, remarks ), and add the correct dispute notation. 
Verification standards demanded : XXXX XXXX ( or any furnisher ) must produce : The original signed cardholder agreement or application bearing the consumers signature/consent ; The complete, itemized payment history proving charges and payments that support the {$8200.00} balance ; A clear itemized charge-off calculation ( principal, fees, interest ) and documentation showing when/how the charge-off was calculated ; The Date of First Delinquency ( DOFD ) with supporting documentation ; The XXXX XXXX record originally transmitted to each bureau XXXX XXXX XXXX, Account Status code, Payment Rating, Compliance Condition Codes, Remarks ), and the subscriber/furnisher identity used in that transmission ; Any evidence of assignment or sale of the account ( chain of title, bill of sale ) if a third party is attempting XXXX collect. 
What does NOT count as verification : do not accept as verification : a copy of my credit report, screenshots, non-specific we verified statements, a note that a credit repair company is working my file, or an assertion of identity theft without documents. Those are insufficient under FCRA 611 ( a ) and XXXX XXXX requirements. 
Alleged violations : failure to perform a reasonable reinvestigation ( FCRA XXXX ), publishing inaccurate/inconsistent XXXX XXXX fields ( FCRA 607 ( b ) ), not marking a disputed item properly, and failing to provide chain-of-custody or debt ownership documentation where required. 
Demand and notice of legal remedies : Provide the full verification packet within 30 days or delete the account from my file. If you fail to properly verify and correct this tradeline, I will escalate filing CFPB/state complaints and pursuing statutory and actual damages under the FCRA ( including litigation against the bureaus and furnishers for willful/negligent noncompliance ). Consider this formal notice : do your job or face enforcement and legal action.

Consumer complaint narrative:
Consumer Reporting Agencies play a crucial role in gathering and assessing consumer credit information. However, they must exercise their responsibilities with fairness, impartiality, and respect for the consumers right to privacy. The banking system relies on accurate and fair credit reporting, and when these reporting methods are unfair, it undermines public trust, which is vital for the system 's stability. I never gave my explicit, written consent for these agencies to report my information, which is a violation of 15 U.S. Code 1681b ( 2 ). This violation has led to an alleged case of identity theft, breaching 15 U.S. Code 1681c-2. The accounts in question are not related to any transactions made by me.

The Fair Debt Collection Practices Act was created to ensure that laws and procedures are in place to protect consumers from abusive, deceptive, and unfair practices by debt collectors. In this case, the company has violated 15 U.S. Code 1692f, which prohibits debt collectors from using unfair or unconscionable means to collect a debt. Specifically, they can not collect any amounts that arent explicitly authorized by the agreement or allowed by law. Additionally, the company has violated 15 U.S. Code 1692e, which prohibits the use of false, deceptive, or misleading representations in debt collection efforts. This includes using deceptive methods to collect a debt or gather consumer information. Moreover, they are forbidden from contacting consumers at inconvenient times or places, or engaging in behavior that harasses or abuses consumers. 

In this case, the company has harassed me by claiming I owe a debt that I do not recognize as my responsibility. They have also contacted me at inconvenient times and places, in violation of the law.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing this complaint because a consumer reporting agency has failed to meet its obligations under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). After disputing inaccurate information on my credit report, I submitted a formal requestpursuant to 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 7 ) and 1681e ( b ) seeking a detailed explanation of the reinvestigation process. Specifically, I requested information describing the steps taken to verify the disputed data, the name, address, and telephone number of the data furnisher, and any documentation relied upon to justify the continued reporting of the item. 

To date, the agency has not provided any of the requested information. This lack of disclosure contravenes the transparency and accountability standards established by the FCRA and raises serious concerns regarding the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of the reinvestigation. The agencys noncompliance may constitute willful or negligent violations under 15 U.S.C. 1681n and 1681o and may expose it to civil liability.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am submitting this complaint to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) to dispute multiple inaccurate and fraudulent items on my credit report. These inaccuracies have caused financial harm, impaired my creditworthiness, and violate my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C. 1681 et seq., including sections 1681i ( a ) ( 1 ) for reinvestigation of disputed information, 1681e ( b ) for accuracy, and 1681g for consumer disclosures. Each item below is inaccurate, incomplete, or misleading, and I demand a full investigation and correction. 

Accounts : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX Balance Owed : {$260.00} Late Payment This late payment is inaccurate and violates FCRA 1681i, which mandates proper verification of disputed items. I have previously provided documentation proving timely payments. Reporting this late payment without verification negatively impacts my creditworthiness, misleads potential lenders, and causes financial harm. XXXX XXXX has failed to provide evidence supporting this late payment. Continued reporting of this inaccurate information is unlawful. Under FCRA, I am entitled to deletion of unverifiable information. I request immediate removal or correction of this late payment. This reporting error falsely affects my credit score and misrepresents my payment history. I have provided statements and confirmations from XXXX XXXX that demonstrate compliance with payment terms. The continued presence of this inaccurate late payment constitutes a violation of federal law. I demand verification or deletion as required under FCRA. Proper investigation should include reviewing payment records, account statements, and communication logs. Misreporting this late payment affects my ability to secure future credit and may influence lenders decisions. Continued reporting also exposes the bureau to legal liability for failing to correct known inaccuracies. I request that this account be corrected or deleted immediately and confirmation provided in writing. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX Balance Owed : {$0.00} Late Payment This account incorrectly shows a late payment, which is a violation of FCRA 611 ( a ) requiring proper investigation of disputed items. I have submitted evidence proving timely payments and compliance with account terms. Reporting an inaccurate late payment misrepresents my credit history and damages my financial credibility. XXXX has failed to provide verification supporting this late payment. Under FCRA 1681i, unverifiable or incorrect information must be removed from the credit report. Failure to correct this account continues to violate federal law. The bureau must investigate using statements, receipts, and official documentation to confirm the accuracy of reported information. Continued reporting of this inaccurate late payment misleads lenders and adversely impacts my financial opportunities. I request immediate deletion or correction of this account. Misreporting late payments is a breach of the FCRA and exposes the bureau to potential legal liability. Proper verification must include thorough review of account history and communication logs. I demand written confirmation once this account is corrected or deleted. Accurate reporting is essential to maintaining fair and lawful credit reporting standards. 

OXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Date Opened : XX/XX/XXXX Balance Owed : {$400.00} Late Payment This late payment is inaccurately reported, violating FCRA 1681i. I have provided evidence proving on-time payments and account compliance. Reporting inaccurate late payments misrepresents my credit history and damages financial reputation. Failure to verify constitutes unlawful reporting. The bureau must review original statements, payment logs, and confirmations. Misreporting harms credit score, lending eligibility, and financial opportunities. Duplicate or unverifiable reporting is prohibited under FCRA 611 ( a ). I request immediate deletion or correction. Continued reporting without verification is a violation of federal law. Proper investigation should include thorough review of account history and supporting documentation. I demand written confirmation once corrected. Misreporting false delinquencies continues to harm my financial profile. I request immediate correction or deletion under FCRA 1681i. 

Inquiry : XXXXXXXX XXXX Inquiry Unauthorized inquiry. I did not authorize XXXX to access my credit report. FCRA 604 ( a ) mandates deletion of all unauthorized inquiries. Reporting this inquiry misrepresents my credit activity and negatively affects my creditworthiness. Continued reporting violates federal law. The bureau must remove this inquiry immediately. I request written confirmation once deleted. Misrepresentation affects lending decisions. Immediate removal is required under FCRA protections. 

I demand that CFPB oversee the full reinvestigation of all disputed addresses and accounts, verify the accuracy of each item, remove any unverifiable or false information, and ensure that all reporting agencies comply with FCRA standards. These errors are affecting my ability to access credit fairly and misrepresent my financial history.

15 U.S. Code 1681s2 - Responsibilities of furnishers of information to consumer reporting agencies ( 3 ) Duty to provide notice of dispute If the completeness or accuracy of any information furnished by any person to any consumer reporting agency is disputed to such person by a consumer, the person may not furnish the information to any consumer reporting agency without notice that such information is disputed by the consumer. 


( B ) Reporting information after notice and confirmation of errors A person shall not furnish information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if ( i ) the person has been notified by the consumer, at the address specified by the person for such notices, that specific information is inaccurate; and ( ii ) the information is, in fact, inaccurate.

Consumer complaint narrative:
Subject : Follow-Up to Chime Complaint : Unauthorized Credit Activity and Misuse of Personal Information. 
I previously submitted complaint regarding misleading marketing and referral tactics used by Chime Financial. But that is not all. 
In addition to the deceptive referral program already indicated in a separate complaint, I am reporting that a credit card account was created under my name without my informed consent. 
I initially signed up for Chime solely to facilitate the transfer of funds between banks, following a referral from a friend. I was led to believe that signing up and providing basic personal information would result in a referral bonus. At no point was I informed, nor did I authorize, Chime to open a credit account under my name, nor did I intend to apply for credit. 
However, Chime created and issued a " Chime XXXX XXXX XXXX credit Card '' in my name without proper disclosure or consent. This account : was reported to the Credit Bureaus as an open line of credit. 
It was issued without any stated credit limit, yet appeared on my credit report, and has negatively impacted my credit score and history. 
These activities are clear violations of the federal regulation under FCRA & FDCPA and etc. Under the : FCRA ( Fair Credit Reporting Act ) 604 Permissible Purpose : Chime had no valid reason to access or report my credit without clear, written consent.

CFPB Regulation Z Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) : Failure to clearly disclose credit terms prior to issuing a credit product. 
UDAAP ( Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ) under the Dodd-Frank Act, 1031 : Misrepresenting a product ( referral-based checking signup ) and using that data to enroll me in an unwanted credit service without full transparency. 
I am requesting the CFPB investigate Chime for : Opening and reporting a credit card account without my express permission, Neglecting to inform me about the impact on my credit report, Using misleading advertising and sign-up processes to obtain and misuse consumer data. 
I would like Chime to : Immediately close the unauthorized credit account, Remove any credit bureau reporting related to it, Provide written confirmation of the removal and corrective action, And be held accountable for deceptive financial practices.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am formally disputing the continued reporting of a closed collection account from LVNV Funding LLC ( account ending in * XXXX ), as listed on my Equifax credit report dated XX/XX/year>, under Confirmation # XXXX. This account is marked as closed yet continues to report an outstanding balance of {$620.00}, which is both misleading and inaccurate. This is not a duplicate dispute, but a new dispute based on updated information now appearing on my credit report, specifically the fact that the account is now labeled closed but still carries a balance. I previously requested documentation to verify the accuracy and validity of this alleged debt. However, despite my request, no evidence was ever provided, and instead, the furnisher proceeded to close the account without supplying any of the required validation materials. I am asserting that this debt is not mine and may be the result of identity theft. Although I am not submitting a police report, I am exercising my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 602A ( 4 ), 611 ( f ), and 609 ( e ), which allow me to request the deletion of unauthorized or fraudulent accounts without providing a police report. The burden of verification remains with the data furnisher and Equifax, and no such verification has occurred in this case. Furthermore, no original creditor has been identified, and no signed contract, transfer agreement, or payment history has been provided to establish a legitimate obligation. Under FCRA 609 ( a ) ( 1 ), I am entitled to receive the method of verification, yet this has been ignored. According to FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( A ), any item that can not be verified must be deleted. The continued reporting of this unresolved, closed account with a balance violates 623 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), which prohibits the furnishing of incomplete or inaccurate consumer information. In addition to these statutory violations, this account is being reported in a manner that is inconsistent with XXXX XXXX compliance standards, the credit industrys official data format. XXXX XXXX XXXX guidelines, a closed collection account should not continue to report a balance, and the account must include fields such as the original creditor and accurate status coding. None of that required formatting appears to be followed here, which means this tradeline is also in violation of FCRA 607 ( b ), requiring maximum possible accuracy in credit reporting. 
Finally, because the balance exceeds {$600.00}, I request confirmation that a XXXX Cancellation of Debt form was filed, as required by the IRS if the debt was settled or XXXX. No such record has been disclosed, which further questions the legality of this continued reporting. Given the above deficiencies, I am demanding the immediate permanent deletion of this account from my Equifax report. This account is unverified, improperly reported, closed without validation, and potentially fraudulent. Please confirm in writing that this account has been removed, or provide documentation showing full verification including the original contract, transfer history, and method of verification used.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I hope this letter finds you in good health and high spirits. I am writing to formally dispute the continuation of an entry on my credit report concerning a XXXX XXXX account with a balance of {$350.00} that remains, inexplicably, on my file despite several unsuccessful attempts at resolution. This account, which has been improperly investigated by the credit bureaus, continues to tarnish my credit report and my financial reputation. 

I am both disappointed and dismayed that despite a number of attempts to remedy this issue, the credit bureaus have failed in their duty under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) to conduct a proper, thorough, and meaningful investigation. Their attempts at validating the account have not only been incomplete but fundamentally flawed. Theyve violated the spirit and letter of consumer protection law by maintaining an inaccurate and unjustified entry on my credit reports. 

According to the FCRA, when a consumer disputes an item on their credit report, the credit bureaus are legally obligated to conduct a reasonable investigation. The mere act of verifying an account does not satisfy this requirement. In my case, the account should never have appeared on my credit report in the first place. Its ongoing presence, especially after several challenges, is not only a stain on my financial standing, but also an utter disregard for the rights I, and every consumer, am afforded under federal law. 

Here are the key points that underscore why this account must be removed immediately : 1. Failure to Properly Investigate : The credit bureaus have done little more than glance at my dispute without any substantial review of the facts, as required by law. Their investigations were neither thorough nor complete. This is an egregious breach of their legal obligations. 

2. Inaccurate Reporting : The continued reporting of this account is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) as the information is erroneous and the result of flawed reporting practices by the creditor. This is a clear violation of the consumer 's right to accurate information under Section 1681e ( b ). 

3. Impact on My Financial Life : This accounts continued presence on my report is not just an inconvenience ; it is actively harming my ability to access credit, loans, and basic financial opportunities. This impact is tangible and can not be ignored.

Product: Credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports
Consumer complaint narrative: I am doing complain by myself and total responsible for this. I am requesting that you immediately delete information off of my credit report that isnt mine. I listed all accounts in Identity Theft. After investigation I found out that all of this account were opened not by me. I have filed a police report stating that these accounts are not mine and that my identity may have been stolen which I have included with this letter. I have listed the accounts below. I do not know how these items ended upon my credit report, but I never signed for or agreed to these charges. I dont know who gave you this information! I would like a copy of whatever says that these accounts are mine, because I assure you, I have never taken out these charges. Is it possible someone forged my name, or that you have confused me with someone else? Please check for me. Even if you receive documents with my signature, please compare them with my real signature ( which is on the police report affidavit ) because if you do you will see I did not sign anything related to these accounts. If you have any questions I would be happy to help. But because these are not mine, please remove them from my credit as soon as possible! The constant judgments awarded against you certainly demonstrate that courts are will not tolerate your bullying tactics.I have made a lawful request of you to verify a report about me in my credit files. I respectfully suggest that you complete your investigation within the time period allotted to you by law, and in the event that you are able to verify this report that you supply me with the name, company address and company phone number of the person having verified the report and a full and complete report on the method you used to perform your investigation as dictated in the Fair Credit Reporting Act. Except as otherwise provided in this section, a consumer reporting agency shall block the reporting of any information in the file of a consumer that the consumer identifies as information that resulted from an alleged identity theft, not later than 4 business days after the date of receipt by such agency of ( 1 ) appropriate proof of the identity of the consumer ; ( 2 ) a copy of an identity theft report ; ( 3 ) the identification of such information by the consumer ; and ( 4 ) a statement by the consumer that the information is not information relating to any transaction by the consumer. ( b ) Notification. A consumer reporting agency shall promptly notify the furnisher of information identified by the consumer under subsection ( a ) of this section ( 1 ) that the information may be a result of identity theft ; ( 2 ) that an identity theft report has been filed ; ( 3 ) that a block has been requested under this section ; and ( 4 ) of the effective dates of the block. ( c ) Authority to decline or rescind. ( 1 ) In general. A consumer reporting agency may decline to block, or may rescind any block, of information relating to a consumer under this section, if the consumer reporting agency reasonably determines that ( A ) the information was blocked in error or a block was requested by the consumer in error ; ( B ) the information was blocked, or a block was requested by the consumer, on the basis of a material misrepresentation of fact by the consumer relevant to the request to block ; or ( C ) the consumer obtained possession of goods, services, or money as a result of the blocked transaction or transactions. ( 2 ) Notification to consumer. If a block of information is declined or rescinded under this subsection, the affected consumer shall be notified promptly, in the same manner as consumers are notified of the reinsertion of information under section 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ) of this title. ( 3 ) Significance of block. For purposes of this subsection, if a consumer reporting agency rescinds a block, the presence of information in the file of a consumer prior to the blocking of such information is not evidence of whether the consumer knew or should have known that the consumer obtained possession of any goods, services, or money as a result of the block. ( d ) Exception for resellers. ( 1 ) No reseller file. This section shall not apply to a consumer reporting agency, if the consumer reporting agency ( A ) is a reseller ; ( B ) is not, at the time of the request of the consumer under subsection ( a ) of this section, otherwise furnishing or reselling a consumer report concerning the information identified by the consumer ; and ( C ) informs the consumer, by any means, that the consumer may report the identity theft to the Bureau to obtain consumer information regarding identity theft. ( 2 ) Reseller with file. The sole obligation of the consumer reporting agency under this section, with regard to any request of a consumer under this section, shall be to block the consumer report maintained by the consumer reporting agency from any subsequent use, if ( A ) the consumer, in accordance with the provisions of subsection ( a ) of this section, identifies, to a consumer reporting agency, information in the file of the consumer that resulted from identity theft ; and ( B ) the consumer reporting agency is a reseller of the identified information. ( 3 ) Notice. In carrying out its obligation under paragraph ( 2 ), the reseller shall promptly provide a notice to the consumer of the decision to block the file. Such notice shall contain the name, address, and telephone number of each consumer reporting agency from which the consumer information was obtained for resale. ( e ) Exception for verification companies. The provisions of this section do not apply to a check services company, acting as such, which issues authorizations for the purpose of approving or processing negotiable instruments, electronic fund transfers, or similar methods of payments, except that, beginning 4 business days after receipt of information described in paragraphs ( 1 ) through ( 3 ) of subsection ( a ) of this section, a check services company shall not report to a national consumer reporting agency described in section 1681a ( p ) of this title, any information identified in the subject identity theft report as resulting from identity theft. ( f ) Access to blocked information by law enforcement agencies. No provision of this section shall be construed as requiring a consumer reporting agency to prevent a Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency from accessing blocked information in a consumer file to which the agency could otherwise obtain access under this subchapter.
